It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure utilized for communication services, including on-premises and cloud-based environments. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates and includes all types of data and information categories related to telecommunications services.
policies.Objective: The objective of the Access Control Policy is to ensure that access to organizational resources is restricted to authorized users only, thereby protecting sensitive information from unauthorized access. Specific goals include achieving compliance with relevant security standards, minimizing the risk of data breaches, and ensuring operational effectiveness through regular access reviews and audits. The policy aims to enhance stakeholder protection by implementing robust access control measures.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the framework for managing user identities and access rights within the organization. It aims to ensure that only authorized individuals have access to sensitive information and systems, thereby protecting organizational assets and complying with relevant regulations. The policy outlines the responsibilities for identity management and the processes for granting, modifying, and revoking access rights.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party vendors who require access to organizational systems and data. It encompasses all IT systems, applications, and infrastructure, including on-premises, cloud, and mobile environments. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to establish a robust identity management framework that ensures secure access to organizational resources. Specific goals include reducing the risk of unauthorized access, ensuring compliance with data protection regulations, and enhancing operational efficiency through streamlined identity processes. Measurable outcomes include a 100% completion rate of access reviews every six months and a reduction in access-related incidents by 50% within the next year.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for managing authentication information to ensure secure access to organizational systems and data. It aims to protect against unauthorized access and data breaches by implementing robust authentication mechanisms. The policy applies to all personnel and systems that handle sensitive information within the organization.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party vendors who access organizational systems and data. It covers all IT systems, applications, and infrastructure, including on-premises, cloud, and mobile environments. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates and includes all types of data classified as sensitive or confidential.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to establish a secure authentication framework that mitigates the risk of unauthorized access to organizational resources. Specific goals include achieving 100% compliance with MFA implementation for critical systems, reducing password-related incidents by 50% within the next year, and ensuring all personnel are trained on secure authentication practices. The policy also aims to meet regulatory requirements related to data protection and access control.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the framework for managing access rights to organizational systems and data. It aims to ensure that access is granted based on the principle of least privilege, thereby protecting sensitive information and maintaining compliance with relevant standards. The policy outlines the responsibilities for access control and the procedures for granting, modifying, and revoking access rights.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, personnel, and third-party vendors who require access to organizational systems and data. It covers all information systems, applications, and infrastructure, including on-premises, cloud, and mobile environments. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that access to sensitive information is restricted to authorized personnel only, thereby mitigating the risk of data breaches. Specific goals include achieving 100% compliance with access control procedures, conducting quarterly access reviews, and ensuring timely reporting of unauthorized access incidents.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for managing information security risks associated with supplier relationships. It aims to ensure that suppliers who have access to sensitive information are capable of protecting that information adequately. The policy also outlines the necessary legal and regulatory compliance measures that must be adhered to when engaging with suppliers.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units that engage with suppliers, including procurement, IT, and legal departments. It covers all systems and applications that handle sensitive information, including cloud-based services and on-premises infrastructure. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates and includes third-party vendors and service providers. It encompasses all types of sensitive data, including PII and telecommunications records.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to mitigate risks associated with supplier relationships by ensuring that all suppliers are capable of protecting sensitive information. Specific goals include achieving compliance with legal and regulatory requirements, establishing clear confidentiality agreements, and ensuring that suppliers implement adequate security measures. The policy aims to protect stakeholder interests and maintain the integrity of sensitive information.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes requirements for addressing information security within supplier agreements to ensure that all third-party relationships comply with organizational security standards. It aims to protect the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of information shared with suppliers, particularly in telecommunications. The policy outlines the necessary terms and conditions that must be included in supplier agreements to mitigate risks associated with third-party services.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units that engage with suppliers, including procurement, IT, and legal departments. It encompasses all telecommunications services and related infrastructure utilized by the organization, covering both on-premises and cloud-based solutions. The policy is applicable to all geographic locations where the organization operates and includes third-party vendors and partners involved in telecommunications services.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all supplier agreements adequately address information security risks, thereby protecting organizational data and telecommunications services. Specific goals include achieving 100% compliance with security requirements in supplier contracts, reducing incidents related to third-party service failures by 50%, and ensuring that all suppliers are vetted for security compliance before engagement.

policies.PolicyName: Information Security Management in ICT Supply Chain
policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for managing information security within the ICT supply chain to protect sensitive customer data and ensure compliance with relevant standards. It outlines the necessary controls and agreements that telecommunications organizations must implement with suppliers to safeguard information integrity and confidentiality. The policy aims to mitigate risks associated with third-party suppliers and ensure that all communications and data handling practices meet established security standards.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all telecommunications organizations, their employees, and third-party suppliers involved in the ICT supply chain. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure utilized in the provision of telecommunications services, including on-premises and cloud environments. The policy also encompasses all sensitive customer data types, including personal identifiers and communication records, and applies to all geographic locations where services are provided.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the protection of sensitive customer information throughout the ICT supply chain by establishing clear security requirements for supplier agreements. It aims to achieve measurable outcomes such as zero data breaches related to supplier activities, compliance with ISO/IEC 27002 standards, and enhanced risk management practices. The policy also seeks to foster trust among stakeholders by ensuring that all sensitive information is handled with the utmost care and security.

policies.PolicyName: Monitoring, Review, and Change Management of Supplier Services
policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the framework for monitoring, reviewing, and managing changes to supplier services to ensure compliance with organizational standards and mitigate risks associated with third-party relationships. It aims to enhance operational effectiveness and maintain the integrity of services provided by suppliers. The policy applies to all supplier engagements and outlines the necessary procedures for oversight and change management.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in supplier management, including procurement, operations, and compliance teams. It covers all supplier services utilized across on-premises, cloud, and remote environments. The policy encompasses all types of suppliers, including third-party vendors and contractors, and includes all data types and information categories handled by these suppliers.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all supplier services are effectively monitored and reviewed to maintain compliance with organizational standards and mitigate risks. Specific goals include achieving a minimum of 95% compliance in supplier audits, ensuring timely documentation of changes within 48 hours, and reducing supplier-related incidents by 20% annually. The policy also aims to protect stakeholder interests by ensuring that supplier services meet quality and security standards.

policies.PolicyName: Information Security for Use of Cloud Services
policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the information security requirements for the use of cloud services within the organization. It aims to protect organizational data and ensure compliance with relevant standards, including ISO/IEC 27002. The policy outlines the necessary controls and responsibilities for securing cloud-based resources and data.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units and personnel utilizing cloud services, including IT, HR, Finance, and Operations. It encompasses all cloud-based applications, infrastructure, and services used across the organization, regardless of geographic location. This policy also covers third-party cloud service providers and any data types processed or stored in the cloud, including sensitive and personal information.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the security of organizational data in cloud environments by implementing robust information security controls. Specific goals include achieving compliance with ISO/IEC 27002, mitigating risks associated with cloud services, and ensuring the protection of sensitive information. The policy aims to establish measurable outcomes such as regular audits of cloud service providers and incident response readiness.

policies.PolicyName: Information Security Incident Management Planning and Preparation
policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the framework for managing information security incidents within the organization. It outlines the necessary planning and preparation steps to ensure effective incident response, including criteria for evaluating incidents and escalation procedures. The policy aims to protect both customer and organizational interests by ensuring timely communication and resolution of incidents.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in incident management, including IT, customer service, and telecommunications departments. It covers all systems and applications used for incident reporting and management, as well as all personnel who interact with customers regarding incident resolution. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates and includes third-party vendors involved in incident response. It encompasses all types of data and information categories related to customer configurations and service delivery.
policies.Objective: The primary objective of this policy is to ensure a proactive approach to information security incident management, aiming for timely identification, evaluation, and resolution of incidents. Specific measurable outcomes include a reduction in incident response times by 20%, improved customer satisfaction ratings regarding incident handling, and compliance with relevant regulatory requirements. The policy also aims to mitigate risks associated with information security incidents and enhance operational effectiveness in incident management.

policies.PolicyName: Assessment and Decision on Information Security Events
policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the framework for assessing and making decisions regarding information security events within the organization. It aims to ensure that all security events are classified appropriately and that their impact on customers is considered, particularly in telecommunications. The policy outlines the responsibilities and procedures for timely and effective response to security incidents.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in information security management, including IT, telecommunications, and customer service departments. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure used for processing and storing sensitive information, including on-premises and cloud environments. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates and includes third-party vendors that handle customer data.
policies.Objective: The primary objective of this policy is to establish a clear and effective process for assessing information security events to minimize risks and protect customer data. Specific goals include ensuring timely classification of security events, implementing appropriate response measures, achieving compliance with ISO/IEC 27002, and enhancing overall operational effectiveness in incident management.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the procedures and responsibilities for responding to information security incidents within the organization. It aims to ensure a swift and effective response to minimize the impact of incidents on organizational operations and customer trust. The policy is applicable to all employees and contractors who handle organizational information and systems.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, employees, contractors, and third-party vendors involved in information handling. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure used for processing organizational data, including on-premises and cloud environments. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to establish a structured approach for responding to information security incidents, ensuring timely reporting and resolution. It aims to minimize operational disruptions, protect sensitive data, and maintain customer trust. The policy also seeks to comply with relevant regulatory requirements and improve the organization's overall security posture through continuous improvement.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the procedures for learning from information security incidents to enhance incident management and organizational resilience. It emphasizes the importance of post-incident analysis and stakeholder engagement to identify lessons learned and improve future responses. The policy applies to all organizational units involved in incident management and aims to foster a culture of continuous improvement.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in information security incident management, including IT, security, and customer service departments. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure utilized by the organization, including on-premises and cloud environments. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates and includes third-party vendors involved in incident response. All types of data related to incidents, including personal and sensitive information, are covered under this policy.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to establish a systematic approach for learning from information security incidents, ensuring that lessons learned are effectively integrated into incident management practices. The policy aims to achieve measurable outcomes such as reduced incident response times, improved stakeholder communication, and enhanced overall security posture. It also seeks to ensure compliance with relevant regulatory requirements and to mitigate risks associated with future incidents.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements and procedures for the systematic collection of evidence in compliance with ISO/IEC 27002 standards. It is essential for ensuring that all evidence collected is reliable, admissible, and properly documented to support investigations and audits. The policy applies to all organizational units involved in evidence collection and management.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party vendors involved in the collection of evidence across all organizational units. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure used for evidence collection, including on-premises and cloud environments. The policy is applicable in all geographic locations where the organization operates and includes all types of data and information categories relevant to investigations.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to establish a standardized approach for the collection of evidence that ensures compliance with ISO/IEC 27002. Specific goals include maintaining the integrity and authenticity of evidence, ensuring proper documentation and storage, and facilitating effective investigations. The policy aims to mitigate risks associated with evidence mishandling and to enhance operational effectiveness in evidence management.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for maintaining information security during periods of disruption, ensuring that organizational data and systems remain protected. It outlines the necessary controls and procedures to mitigate risks associated with disruptions, including natural disasters, cyber incidents, and other unforeseen events. The policy is essential for safeguarding sensitive information and maintaining operational continuity.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, including IT, HR, and Operations, covering all systems, applications, and infrastructure utilized by the organization. It encompasses all geographic locations where the organization operates, including on-premises, cloud, and remote environments. Third-party vendors and partners that handle organizational data are also included in this scope. The policy covers all types of data, including personal, sensitive, and proprietary information.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that the organization can effectively respond to disruptions while maintaining the security of its information assets. Specific goals include minimizing data loss, ensuring rapid recovery of systems, and maintaining compliance with relevant regulations. The policy aims to enhance operational resilience and protect stakeholder interests during disruptive events.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for ensuring ICT readiness to support business continuity in the event of disruptions. It emphasizes the importance of maintaining telecommunications services and outlines the necessary planning and implementation steps to ensure operational resilience. The policy aims to protect organizational assets and ensure the continuity of critical services during emergencies.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all telecommunications organizations, including their personnel, systems, and infrastructure involved in service delivery. It encompasses all operational environments, including on-premises, cloud, and remote operations. The policy covers all telecommunications services provided to customers and includes third-party vendor relationships that support these services.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that telecommunications organizations are prepared to maintain service availability during disruptions. Specific goals include the establishment of a business continuity plan that meets regulatory compliance, the implementation of effective risk mitigation strategies, and the assurance of essential communications for customers. The policy aims to achieve measurable outcomes such as reduced downtime and improved response times during emergencies.

policies.PolicyName: Legal, Statutory, Regulatory and Contractual Compliance Policy
policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the framework for ensuring compliance with all applicable legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual obligations. It is crucial for maintaining organizational integrity, protecting stakeholder interests, and mitigating legal risks. The policy outlines the responsibilities and processes for identifying, assessing, and adhering to these requirements across all business functions.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, including but not limited to legal, compliance, finance, and operations. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure utilized by the organization, including on-premises and cloud environments. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates and includes third-party vendors and partners involved in contractual agreements. It encompasses all data types and information categories relevant to legal compliance.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that the organization meets all legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual obligations, thereby minimizing legal risks and enhancing operational effectiveness. Specific goals include achieving 100% compliance with identified requirements, conducting annual compliance audits, and providing training to all relevant personnel on compliance matters. The policy aims to protect stakeholder interests and maintain the organization's reputation.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the framework for the protection and management of intellectual property rights within the organization. It aims to ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations while safeguarding the organization's innovations, trademarks, copyrights, and patents. The policy outlines responsibilities and procedures for identifying, protecting, and enforcing intellectual property rights.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, employees, contractors, and third-party vendors involved in the creation, use, or management of intellectual property. It covers all forms of intellectual property including patents, trademarks, copyrights, and trade secrets across all operational environments, including on-premises, cloud, and remote work settings.
policies.Objective: The primary objective of this policy is to protect the organization's intellectual property rights and ensure compliance with relevant laws and regulations. Specific goals include reducing the risk of intellectual property theft, enhancing employee awareness regarding intellectual property rights, and establishing clear procedures for reporting and addressing potential infringements.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for the protection of organizational records to ensure their confidentiality, integrity, and availability. It is essential for compliance with applicable regulations and standards, including ISO/IEC 27002. The policy outlines the responsibilities and procedures for safeguarding records against unauthorized access, alteration, and destruction.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, employees, contractors, and third-party vendors who handle records. subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for managing access to telecommunications systems and data to ensure that only authorized personnel have access to sensitive information. It includes user authentication, authorization processes, and access review procedures.

WHEN: Access reviews must be conducted quarterly. HOW: Use automated tools to monitor access logs and generate reports. WHERE: This control applies to all telecommunications systems and applications.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the procedures for responding to information security incidents within telecommunications organizations. It includes incident detection, reporting, response, and recovery processes.

WHERE: This subpolicy applies to all organizational units involved in incident management.

WHEN: Access rights must be reviewed quarterly and updated as necessary. HOW: Use automated tools to monitor and log access attempts and changes to access rights. WHERE: This control applies to all telecommunications systems and applications handling sensitive information.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the procedures for responding to information security incidents within telecommunications organizations. It includes incident detection, reporting, response, and recovery processes to minimize the impact of security breaches.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish an incident response team (IRT) to handle security incidents. WHO: The IRT will consist of members from IT, Legal, and Communications departments. WHO: The Information Security Officer is responsible for overseeing the risk assessment process. subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the procedures for responding to information security incidents, including detection, reporting, and recovery processes. It aims to minimize the impact of security breaches on organizational operations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the access control measures for auditors during testing to protect sensitive information and maintain system integrity.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for protecting the confidentiality of telecommunications information from unauthorized disclosure. HOW: Utilize role-based access control (RBAC) and encryption technologies to secure data. WHERE: Applicable to all telecommunications systems and data storage locations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the measures required to ensure the integrity of telecommunications information, including the authenticity and accuracy of data transmitted across networks.

WHO: IT security team and network administrators. WHEN: Integrity checks must be performed after any system update or installation. subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the requirements for ensuring the availability of telecommunications services, particularly during emergencies and high-demand situations.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and implement a business continuity plan that prioritizes essential communications. WHO: Business continuity planning team and operations management. WHEN: The plan must be tested annually and updated as necessary. HOW: Conduct regular drills and simulations to ensure readiness. WHERE: Applies to all operational environments, including on-premises and cloud-based systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting regular risk assessments to identify and mitigate information security risks within telecommunications operations. subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the process for submitting new terms and abbreviations for approval to ensure they are properly defined and documented within the Custom Policy Framework.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for controlling access to information systems and data within telecommunications organizations. WHEN: Access reviews must be conducted quarterly. HOW: Use access control software to monitor and manage user permissions. WHERE: Applies to all information systems and applications within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy provides guidelines for responding to information security incidents within telecommunications organizations. It establishes procedures for identifying, reporting, and managing security incidents to minimize impact and recover operations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the measures for protecting sensitive data, including personally identifiable information (PII) and confidential business data, from unauthorized access and breaches.

WHEN: Data protection measures must be reviewed annually. HOW: Conduct regular audits to ensure compliance with data protection standards. WHERE: Applies to all data storage and transmission systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for controlling access to telecommunications systems and information assets to prevent unauthorized access and modifications.

WHO: Incident Response Team will lead the response efforts. WHEN: The plan must be tested annually and updated as necessary. HOW: Incidents will be documented and analyzed to improve future responses. WHERE: This applies to all telecommunications operations and systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the measures to protect telecommunications systems from malware threats, including viruses and worms.

WHERE: This control applies to all end systems and communications infrastructure.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the specific roles and responsibilities of personnel involved in information security management, ensuring clarity and accountability in the execution of security measures.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Define and document roles and responsibilities for all personnel involved in information security. WHO: Information Security Officer, HR Department, Department Heads. WHEN: Annually or upon changes in personnel. HOW: Use a formalized role definition document and training sessions to communicate responsibilities. subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for training and awareness programs related to information security for all employees and contractors.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a training program that covers information security policies, procedures, and best practices. WHO: Information Security Officer, Training Department. WHEN: At onboarding and annually thereafter. HOW: Conduct training sessions, workshops, and assessments to ensure understanding. WHERE: All organizational locations and remote environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the implementation of role-based access controls to ensure that access to information systems is granted based on defined roles.

WHEN: At the time of user account creation and during access reviews. HOW: Use access control management tools to assign and monitor access rights. subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the process for regularly reviewing and revoking access rights to ensure compliance with access control policies.

WHO: The procurement department is responsible for ensuring these clauses are included in all supplier agreements. WHEN: This requirement must be fulfilled prior to the signing of any supplier agreement. HOW: The procurement team will utilize a standard template that includes these clauses and will conduct regular reviews of supplier agreements. subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the procedures for identifying, reporting, and responding to information security incidents involving customer data. It establishes criteria for escalation and communication with affected customers.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Telecommunications organizations must establish procedures for classifying and responding to information security incidents. WHO: The incident response team is responsible for managing incidents and communicating with customers. WHEN: Incidents must be reported immediately upon identification, with escalation procedures activated if service levels are not met. HOW: The organization will implement a ticketing system to track incidents and ensure timely communication. WHERE: This applies to all telecommunications systems and customer service operations.

subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document and maintain operating procedures for information security. WHO: Information Security Officer and relevant department heads. WHEN: Procedures must be reviewed and updated annually or as needed. HOW: Utilize a centralized documentation system for version control and accessibility. WHERE: All organizational units handling sensitive information.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the procedures for responding to spam incidents within telecommunications services. It outlines the responsibilities of the organization in managing spam complaints and implementing necessary controls.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and implement policies for responding to spam incidents. WHO: Telecommunications Compliance Team. WHEN: Policies must be reviewed bi-annually or as incidents arise. HOW: Monitor spam reports and take corrective actions as necessary. WHERE: All telecommunications services provided by the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for responding to Denial of Service (DoS) and Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks. It specifies the actions to be taken to mitigate the impact of such attacks on telecommunications services.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish and implement response procedures for DoS/DDoS attacks. WHO: Network Security Team. WHEN: Procedures must be tested quarterly and updated as needed. HOW: Utilize monitoring tools to detect and respond to attacks promptly. WHERE: All telecommunications infrastructure and services.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for filtering malicious traffic and managing network resources during DoS/DDoS attacks. It specifies the technical measures to be implemented to protect telecommunications facilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement packet filtering and traffic management protocols to identify and block malicious traffic. WHO: Network security team is responsible for monitoring and managing traffic. WHEN: Continuous monitoring with immediate action during detected attacks. HOW: Utilize intrusion detection systems (IDS) and firewalls to filter traffic. WHERE: Applicable to all network entry points and telecommunications facilities.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the process for suspending services to customers identified as sources of DoS/DDoS attacks to protect overall network integrity.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Suspend telecommunications services to identified customers contributing to DoS/DDoS attacks. WHO: Customer service and network operations teams will execute suspensions. WHEN: Immediate action upon identification of the customer as a source of attack. HOW: Follow established protocols for service suspension and customer notification. WHERE: Applicable to all customer accounts within the telecommunications organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for establishing and managing confidentiality agreements to protect sensitive communications.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and enforce confidentiality agreements for all personnel handling sensitive communications. WHO: Legal and compliance teams are responsible for drafting and managing agreements. WHEN: Agreements must be established prior to access to sensitive information. HOW: Utilize standardized templates and review processes for agreements. WHERE: Applicable to all employees and contractors with access to telecommunications data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the protocols for retaining and deleting telecommunications data to comply with legal requirements and protect customer privacy.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish data retention periods and deletion protocols for telecommunications data. WHO: Data management team is responsible for implementing retention schedules. WHEN: Data must be retained only for the necessary period and deleted immediately after. HOW: Utilize automated data management tools to enforce retention policies. WHERE: Applicable to all data storage systems within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for prioritizing and managing communications during emergencies.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement protocols for prioritizing essential communications during emergencies. WHO: Emergency response team is responsible for executing protocols. WHEN: Protocols must be activated immediately upon identification of an emergency. HOW: Utilize communication management systems to facilitate priority messaging. WHERE: Applicable to all telecommunications facilities and operational environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for establishing agreements with external agencies to ensure effective communication during emergencies.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish agreements with relevant organizations for essential communication prioritization. WHO: Legal and compliance teams are responsible for drafting agreements. WHEN: Agreements must be in place prior to emergency situations. HOW: Conduct regular reviews and updates of agreements. WHERE: Applicable to all inter-agency communication efforts.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for coordinating information security incidents within telecommunications organizations. This coordination should occur immediately upon identification of an incident (WHEN), utilizing established communication channels (HOW) to ensure timely and effective responses. The scope of this control includes all organizational units involved in incident management (WHERE), ensuring that all actions taken are necessary and sufficient for addressing the incident (WHAT).

HOW: Use industry-standard security measures and conduct regular audits to ensure effectiveness. WHERE: All organizational facilities, particularly telecommunications centers and data centers.

subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement visitor logging and access control measures for all entry points. WHO: Reception staff and security personnel are responsible for managing visitor access. WHEN: Visitor logs must be maintained in real-time and reviewed weekly. HOW: Use electronic logging systems to record visitor data and ensure it is stored securely. WHERE: All entry points to telecommunications operation rooms and control centers.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for securing all organizational offices, rooms, and facilities to prevent unauthorized access and protect sensitive information.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Ensure all offices and facilities are secured with access controls and monitoring systems. WHO: Facility managers and security teams are responsible for implementation. WHEN: Security measures must be in place within 6 months of policy approval. HOW: Conduct regular security assessments and install access control systems. WHERE: All organizational offices and facilities.

WHEN: Assessments must be conducted annually and after any significant environmental event. HOW: Develop and implement disaster recovery plans and environmental controls. subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for access control to ensure that only authorized personnel have access to sensitive information and systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy provides detailed requirements for conducting information security risk assessments within telecommunications organizations. It outlines the processes for identifying, analyzing, and mitigating risks associated with information assets.

WHERE: Applies to all relevant operational units and personnel involved in network management.

subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement automated systems to monitor network traffic and detect congestion. WHO: Network Operations Team is responsible for monitoring and reporting. WHEN: Continuous monitoring with daily reports on congestion status. HOW: Utilize network management tools to analyze traffic data and identify congestion points. WHERE: Applicable to all telecommunications facilities and systems handling essential communications.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the strategies for improving temporary throughput during congestion events, including the use of distributed processing centers and adaptive configurations.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement distributed processing centers and adaptive configuration changes to enhance throughput. WHO: IT Infrastructure Team is responsible for implementation and adjustments. WHEN: As needed during identified congestion events or when performance thresholds are met. HOW: Employ load balancing techniques and configure systems to dynamically allocate resources. WHERE: Applicable to all operational environments, including cloud and on-premises systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for configuring and managing firewalls to protect the network perimeter. It includes guidelines for rule creation, monitoring, and regular updates to firewall settings to adapt to emerging threats.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Configure firewalls to restrict unauthorized access and monitor traffic. WHO: Network Security Team is responsible for configuration and management. WHEN: Firewalls must be reviewed and updated bi-annually or as needed based on threat intelligence. HOW: Utilize automated tools for monitoring and reporting firewall activity. WHERE: Applies to all organizational network entry points, including remote access and cloud environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the implementation and management of Intrusion Detection and Prevention Systems to identify and respond to potential network threats in real-time.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Deploy IDPS to monitor network traffic for suspicious activity. WHO: IT Security Analysts are responsible for monitoring and responding to alerts. WHEN: IDPS must be monitored continuously, with weekly reports generated for review. HOW: Utilize established protocols for incident response based on IDPS alerts. WHERE: Applies to all network segments, including internal and external traffic.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates regular training for all employees on network security best practices to reduce the risk of human error leading to network vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct security awareness training sessions for all employees. WHO: HR and IT Security Teams are responsible for organizing and delivering training. WHEN: Training must be conducted quarterly, with additional sessions for new hires. HOW: Use a combination of online modules and in-person workshops to deliver content. WHERE: Training applies to all employees and contractors accessing the network.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for implementing traffic management tools and monitoring systems to detect and respond to network congestion. It includes guidelines for the configuration of network devices to prioritize critical traffic and the establishment of thresholds for alerting personnel to congestion events.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement traffic management tools and monitoring systems to analyze network traffic patterns and detect congestion. WHO: Network Operations Team is responsible for the implementation and monitoring. WHEN: Continuous monitoring with weekly performance reviews. HOW: Utilize network monitoring software to track traffic patterns and configure alerts for congestion thresholds. WHERE: Applicable to all network segments within the organization, including on-premises and cloud environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the procedures for responding to incidents of network congestion that may pose security risks. It includes steps for identifying, reporting, and mitigating congestion-related incidents to ensure minimal disruption to services.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish incident response procedures for network congestion incidents. WHO: Incident Response Team and Network Operations Team are responsible for execution. WHEN: Procedures must be activated immediately upon detection of congestion incidents. HOW: Follow the established incident response plan, including escalation protocols and communication strategies. WHERE: Applicable to all network environments and systems affected by congestion.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for the configuration, management, and monitoring of user endpoint devices to ensure compliance with security standards. It includes guidelines for device setup, software installation, and ongoing maintenance.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Endpoint devices must be configured with approved security settings and software. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for configuration and management. WHEN: Initial setup must occur before device deployment, with quarterly reviews for compliance. HOW: Use automated tools for configuration management and monitoring. WHERE: Applies to all organizational endpoint devices, including those used remotely.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the procedures for reporting security incidents related to user endpoint devices. It ensures timely response and mitigation of potential threats.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Users must report any security incidents involving endpoint devices immediately. WHO: All personnel using endpoint devices are responsible for reporting. WHEN: Reports must be made within 24 hours of incident discovery. HOW: Use the organization's incident reporting system to document and escalate incidents. WHERE: Applies to all locations where endpoint devices are used.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for granting privileged access rights to personnel, ensuring that access is based on a documented need-to-know basis and approved by the appropriate authority.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Access rights must be granted through a formal request process that includes justification for the need for privileged access. WHO: The IT Security Manager is responsible for reviewing and approving access requests. WHEN: Access requests must be submitted prior to the need for access and reviewed within 5 business days. HOW: Access requests must be documented in the access management system and include approval signatures. WHERE: This procedure applies to all systems and applications requiring privileged access.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the process for conducting regular reviews of privileged access rights to ensure compliance with the policy and to identify any unauthorized access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: A quarterly review of all privileged access rights must be conducted to verify that access is still required. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for conducting the reviews and reporting findings. WHEN: Reviews must occur at the end of each quarter. HOW: Reviews will be documented, and any discrepancies must be addressed within 10 business days. WHERE: This procedure applies to all systems and applications where privileged access is granted.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the steps for revoking privileged access rights when personnel no longer require access due to termination or change in job responsibilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Access rights must be revoked immediately upon termination or change in job role. WHO: The HR department must notify the IT Security Team of any personnel changes. WHEN: Revocation must occur within 24 hours of notification. HOW: Revocation actions must be documented in the access management system. WHERE: This procedure applies to all systems and applications where privileged access is granted.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for granting, modifying, and revoking access rights to sensitive information. It includes requirements for user authentication, role-based access control, and periodic access reviews.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement role-based access control to ensure users have access only to the information necessary for their job functions. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for managing user access rights. WHEN: Access rights must be reviewed quarterly and updated as necessary. HOW: Access rights will be managed through an automated identity management system that logs all changes. WHERE: This control applies to all systems and applications that store or process sensitive information.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the requirements for logging access to sensitive information and monitoring for unauthorized access attempts. It includes guidelines for incident reporting and response.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All access to sensitive information must be logged, and any unauthorized access attempts must trigger an alert. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for monitoring access logs and responding to incidents. WHEN: Logs must be reviewed daily, and incidents must be reported immediately. HOW: Access logs will be maintained in a secure logging system that supports real-time monitoring. WHERE: This control applies to all systems that handle sensitive information.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for requesting, granting, and revoking access to source code repositories. It includes the roles and responsibilities of personnel involved in the access management process and the documentation required for each access request.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Access requests must be submitted through the designated access management system. WHO: The development team lead is responsible for approving access requests. WHEN: Access requests must be reviewed and approved within 5 business days. HOW: Access will be granted using role-based access control mechanisms. WHERE: This control applies to all source code repositories managed by the organization, both on-premises and in the cloud.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) across all critical systems and applications. It specifies the types of authentication factors to be used and the processes for user enrollment and management.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement multi-factor authentication for all users accessing critical systems. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for implementation and oversight. WHEN: MFA must be deployed within 60 days of policy approval and reviewed annually. HOW: Use industry-standard MFA solutions and conduct user training sessions. WHERE: Applicable to all organizational systems classified as critical, including cloud services and on-premises applications.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the requirements for password creation, management, and expiration to ensure strong password practices across the organization.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish strong password requirements, including length, complexity, and expiration. WHO: All users are responsible for adhering to password guidelines, with the IT Security Team overseeing compliance. WHEN: Password policies must be enforced immediately upon policy approval and reviewed every 6 months. HOW: Utilize automated tools to enforce password policies and conduct periodic audits. WHERE: Applies to all systems requiring user authentication.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for monitoring resource utilization and generating reports to inform decision-making regarding capacity management. It includes the establishment of key performance indicators (KPIs) and regular reporting schedules.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement monitoring tools to track resource utilization and generate reports. WHO: IT Operations Team is responsible for monitoring and reporting. WHEN: Reports must be generated monthly and reviewed quarterly. HOW: Utilize automated monitoring tools and dashboards to collect and analyze data. WHERE: Applies to all IT systems and applications within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the processes for capacity planning and forecasting future resource needs based on business growth and usage trends. It includes methodologies for analyzing historical data and predicting future demands.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct capacity planning sessions to analyze historical data and forecast future needs. WHO: Capacity Management Team is responsible for planning and forecasting. WHEN: Capacity planning must occur bi-annually, with adjustments made as necessary. HOW: Use statistical analysis and modeling techniques to predict future capacity requirements. WHERE: Applies to all business units and relevant systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for the installation and maintenance of antivirus software on all organizational devices. It specifies the types of antivirus solutions to be used, update frequencies, and monitoring procedures.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Install and maintain approved antivirus software on all organizational devices. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for implementation and maintenance. WHEN: Antivirus software must be installed on all devices prior to deployment and updated weekly. HOW: Use automated tools to ensure software is up-to-date and conduct monthly reviews of antivirus logs. WHERE: This control applies to all organizational devices, including desktops, laptops, and servers.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates regular training sessions for all employees on malware awareness, prevention strategies, and reporting procedures. It aims to enhance the organization's overall security posture by educating personnel on the risks associated with malware.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct mandatory training sessions on malware awareness for all employees. WHO: HR and IT Security Team will coordinate training sessions. WHEN: Training must be conducted bi-annually, with additional sessions for new hires. HOW: Utilize online training modules and in-person workshops to deliver content. WHERE: Training applies to all employees across all organizational units.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the procedures for reporting suspected malware incidents and outlines the response actions to be taken by the IT security team. It ensures timely detection and mitigation of malware threats.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a clear process for reporting suspected malware incidents. WHO: All employees must report incidents to the IT Security Team. WHEN: Reports must be made immediately upon suspicion of malware presence. HOW: Use a designated incident reporting tool to log and track incidents. WHERE: This process applies to all organizational systems and environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting regular vulnerability scans and assessments of all organizational systems and applications. It specifies the tools, frequency, and reporting mechanisms to ensure effective identification of vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct vulnerability scans using approved tools on a quarterly basis. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for executing scans. WHEN: Scans shall be performed quarterly and after any significant system changes. HOW: Utilize automated scanning tools and manual assessments as necessary. WHERE: All organizational systems, including on-premises and cloud environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the process for prioritizing and remediating identified vulnerabilities based on risk assessment. It includes timelines for remediation and escalation procedures for critical vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Remediate identified vulnerabilities within defined timelines based on risk priority. WHO: IT Security Team and System Owners are responsible for remediation actions. WHEN: Critical vulnerabilities must be addressed within 24 hours, high within 7 days, and medium within 30 days. HOW: Implement patches, configuration changes, or other mitigation strategies as appropriate. WHERE: Applies to all systems and applications identified in the vulnerability assessment.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for reporting and monitoring vulnerabilities, including documentation and communication protocols to stakeholders.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document and report all identified vulnerabilities and remediation efforts to management. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for reporting. WHEN: Reports shall be generated monthly and after significant incidents. HOW: Utilize a centralized vulnerability management system for tracking and reporting. WHERE: Applies to all organizational units involved in vulnerability management.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for managing changes to system configurations, including the approval process, documentation requirements, and monitoring of changes. It ensures that all changes are systematically reviewed and authorized to prevent unauthorized modifications.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a formal change control process for all configuration changes. WHO: Configuration Management Team is responsible for reviewing and approving changes. WHEN: Changes must be submitted for review at least 5 business days prior to implementation. HOW: Utilize a change management tool to document and track all changes, including approvals and implementation dates. WHERE: This control applies to all information systems and configurations managed by the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific procedures for securely deleting data from all organizational systems. It includes guidelines for identifying data for deletion, selecting appropriate deletion methods, and documenting the deletion process.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement secure deletion methods such as data wiping, degaussing, or physical destruction of storage media. WHO: IT Department is responsible for executing data deletion. WHEN: Data deletion must occur immediately after the retention period expires or when data is no longer needed. HOW: Use approved data deletion tools and maintain logs of deletion activities. WHERE: Applies to all organizational systems, including on-premises and cloud environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific techniques and processes for implementing data masking across all relevant systems and applications. It includes guidelines for selecting appropriate masking methods based on data sensitivity and usage context.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement data masking techniques such as tokenization, encryption, and obfuscation for all sensitive data. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for the implementation and monitoring of data masking techniques. WHEN: Data masking must be applied during the data lifecycle, specifically at the point of data entry and prior to any data sharing or processing. HOW: Utilize approved data masking tools and methodologies, and conduct regular reviews to ensure effectiveness. WHERE: This control applies to all systems and applications that process sensitive data, including cloud and on-premises environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for encrypting sensitive data both at rest and in transit. All sensitive data must be encrypted using industry-standard encryption algorithms to prevent unauthorized access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement encryption for all sensitive data at rest and in transit. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for encryption implementation. WHEN: Encryption must be applied before any sensitive data is stored or transmitted. HOW: Use AES-256 encryption for data at rest and TLS 1.2 or higher for data in transit. WHERE: Applies to all systems and applications that handle sensitive data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the access control measures required to limit access to sensitive data. Access must be granted based on the principle of least privilege.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement role-based access controls (RBAC) to restrict access to sensitive data. WHO: Department Managers are responsible for defining access roles. WHEN: Access reviews must be conducted quarterly. HOW: Use access control lists (ACLs) to enforce RBAC. WHERE: Applies to all systems and applications that store or process sensitive data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for monitoring data access and responding to potential data leakage incidents. Continuous monitoring must be implemented to detect unauthorized access attempts.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement continuous monitoring tools to detect unauthorized access to sensitive data. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for monitoring and incident response. WHEN: Monitoring must be conducted in real-time, with incident response initiated within 1 hour of detection. HOW: Use SIEM tools to analyze access logs and alerts. WHERE: Applies to all systems that handle sensitive data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific requirements for the frequency of backups and the retention period for backup data. It ensures that backups are performed consistently and that historical data is retained for compliance and recovery purposes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Backups must be performed daily for all critical systems and weekly for non-critical systems. Retention of backup data must be maintained for a minimum of 90 days. WHO: The IT department is responsible for executing and monitoring backup processes. WHEN: Backups must be completed by 11:59 PM daily and weekly backups by Sunday at 11:59 PM. HOW: Automated backup solutions must be utilized to ensure compliance with the schedule. WHERE: Backups must be stored in secure off-site locations and cloud storage solutions.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the procedures for testing and validating backup data to ensure that it can be restored successfully. Regular testing is essential to confirm the integrity of backup data and the effectiveness of the backup process.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Backup data must be tested for recoverability at least quarterly. WHO: The IT department is responsible for conducting the tests and documenting the results. WHEN: Testing must occur within the first week of each quarter. HOW: A sample of backup data must be restored to a test environment to verify integrity and accessibility. WHERE: Testing must be conducted in a controlled environment separate from production systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific requirements for implementing redundancy measures in information processing facilities, including hardware, software, and network components. It mandates the establishment of backup systems and failover protocols to ensure continuous service availability.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement backup systems and failover protocols for all critical information processing facilities. WHO: IT Operations Team is responsible for implementation and maintenance. WHEN: Redundancy measures must be established within 30 days of system deployment and reviewed annually. HOW: Use industry-standard tools and procedures for backup and failover testing. WHERE: Applies to all data centers, cloud environments, and remote processing locations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific retention periods for various types of telecommunications data and the procedures for secure deletion once the retention period has expired or the data is no longer needed.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish and document retention periods for telecommunications data based on legal and business requirements. WHO: The Compliance Officer is responsible for defining and updating retention schedules. WHEN: Retention periods must be reviewed annually or upon changes in legal requirements. HOW: Utilize a data management system to track retention periods and automate deletion processes. WHERE: This applies to all telecommunications data stored in organizational systems, including cloud and on-premises environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific controls to be implemented for effective monitoring of organizational activities. It includes guidelines for the selection of monitoring tools, data collection methods, and reporting procedures to ensure compliance with the Monitoring Activities Policy.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement monitoring tools and processes to track system performance and security incidents. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for the implementation and oversight of monitoring activities. WHEN: Monitoring activities shall be conducted continuously, with weekly reviews of collected data. HOW: Utilize automated monitoring tools to collect data and generate reports for analysis. WHERE: Monitoring shall be applied across all organizational systems, including on-premises and cloud environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for configuring and managing Network Time Protocol (NTP) services across all organizational systems to ensure consistent and accurate time synchronization.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Configure all systems to use NTP for time synchronization. WHO: IT department is responsible for the configuration and management of NTP services. WHEN: NTP configurations must be reviewed and updated quarterly or upon significant changes to the network. HOW: Use automated tools to monitor NTP synchronization status and generate alerts for deviations. WHERE: This control applies to all servers, workstations, and network devices within the organization.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Access Control for Privileged Utility Programs
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the access control measures for privileged utility programs, including authorization processes, user roles, and monitoring requirements. It specifies the criteria for granting access and the responsibilities of users in maintaining security.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement access control measures for privileged utility programs, including role-based access and logging of all usage. WHO: Access will be granted by the IT Security Manager to authorized personnel only. WHEN: Access requests must be reviewed and approved prior to granting access, with periodic reviews conducted every six months. HOW: Access will be managed through an identity and access management system that logs all usage and alerts on unauthorized attempts. WHERE: This control applies to all systems and applications where privileged utility programs are utilized.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for testing software changes and establishing rollback procedures to ensure operational continuity and system integrity.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a structured testing process for all software changes, including regression testing and rollback procedures. WHO: IT department and authorized maintenance personnel. WHEN: Prior to any software installation or update. HOW: Utilize a dedicated test environment to validate software functionality and performance, and document all testing results. WHERE: Applicable to all operational systems and environments where software changes are made.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for retaining multiple generations of sensitive software to ensure recovery options are available.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Retain at least three generations of sensitive application software. WHO: IT department and software management teams. WHEN: Continuously, with regular audits to ensure compliance. HOW: Maintain a version control system that tracks software versions and retention schedules. WHERE: All sensitive operational systems and applications.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the authorization process for software installation on sensitive systems to prevent unauthorized access and changes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Only authorized personnel may install software on sensitive systems. WHO: Designated IT staff and maintenance personnel. WHEN: At the time of installation or update. HOW: Implement an access control system that restricts installation rights to authorized users only. WHERE: All sensitive operational systems and terminals used for administration.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for controlling access to the organization's network resources. It specifies the authentication methods, user roles, and access permissions necessary to ensure that only authorized personnel can access sensitive network areas.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) for all network resources. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for managing access permissions. WHEN: Access reviews will be conducted quarterly. HOW: Access logs will be monitored and analyzed for unauthorized access attempts. WHERE: This control applies to all networked systems and applications within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the procedures for monitoring network traffic and responding to security incidents. It includes guidelines for the use of monitoring tools and the escalation process for detected threats.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Deploy network monitoring tools to detect anomalies and potential threats. WHO: Network Operations Center (NOC) staff will monitor network traffic. WHEN: Continuous monitoring will be conducted 24/7. HOW: Alerts will be generated for suspicious activities, and incidents will be logged for further investigation. WHERE: This control applies to all organizational networks and connected devices.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the operational security measures required for the management and provisioning of network services. It includes guidelines for securing operations, administration, maintenance, and configuration of network services.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement operational security measures for network services, including access controls, monitoring, and incident response protocols. WHO: IT Security Team and Network Administrators are responsible for implementation. WHEN: Security measures must be reviewed and updated quarterly or upon significant changes to network services. HOW: Utilize security tools and monitoring systems to enforce controls and conduct regular audits. WHERE: Applies to all network services across organizational units and third-party vendors.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for the segregation of production and management networks to prevent unauthorized access and ensure data integrity. It specifies the technical and administrative controls necessary to maintain this segregation.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement firewalls and access control lists to restrict access between production and management networks. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for implementing and monitoring these controls. WHEN: Controls must be established prior to network deployment and reviewed quarterly. HOW: Use network segmentation tools and regular audits to verify compliance. WHERE: Applies to all organizational network segments, including on-premises and cloud environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for ensuring that hosted customer networks are adequately segregated from other operational networks. It includes guidelines for data handling and access controls.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish dedicated virtual LANs (VLANs) for customer networks and enforce strict access controls. WHO: Network Administration Team is responsible for configuration and monitoring. WHEN: Segregation must be implemented during the initial setup of customer networks and reviewed bi-annually. HOW: Utilize network monitoring tools to detect unauthorized access attempts. WHERE: Applies to all customer-facing network segments and associated data storage systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the implementation requirements for web filtering technologies within the organization. It specifies the processes for selecting, deploying, and maintaining web filtering solutions to ensure effective monitoring and control of internet access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement web filtering technologies to restrict access to inappropriate websites. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for the implementation and maintenance of web filtering solutions. WHEN: Web filtering solutions must be deployed within 30 days of policy approval and reviewed quarterly. HOW: Use industry-standard web filtering tools and regularly update filtering criteria based on emerging threats. WHERE: This control applies to all organizational networks, including on-premises and remote access environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the criteria for selecting cryptographic algorithms and protocols to ensure they meet industry standards and organizational requirements. It includes guidelines for evaluating the strength and appropriateness of cryptographic methods used in various applications.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Evaluate and select cryptographic algorithms based on industry standards such as NIST and ISO/IEC 27002. WHO: The Information Security Officer is responsible for overseeing the selection process. WHEN: Algorithm reviews must occur annually or whenever a significant change in technology occurs. HOW: Use a standardized evaluation framework to assess algorithms. WHERE: This control applies to all systems and applications that utilize cryptography within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the process for identifying and documenting security requirements during the initial phases of software development. It mandates collaboration between development teams and security experts to ensure comprehensive coverage of security needs.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct security requirements workshops to identify potential threats and security needs. WHO: Development teams, security officers, and project managers. WHEN: At the beginning of each software development project. HOW: Utilize threat modeling techniques and security frameworks to guide discussions. WHERE: In project kickoff meetings and planning sessions.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the adoption of secure coding standards and practices to prevent common vulnerabilities in software applications. It includes training for developers on secure coding techniques and regular code reviews.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement secure coding standards and conduct code reviews. WHO: Development teams and code reviewers. WHEN: Throughout the development process and at each code commit. HOW: Use automated tools for static code analysis and peer review processes. WHERE: In the development environment and during code integration phases.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes requirements for security testing of software applications before deployment. It includes penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, and compliance checks.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Perform security testing on all applications prior to release. WHO: Security testing teams and external auditors. WHEN: Before the final deployment of software. HOW: Utilize automated testing tools and manual testing techniques to identify vulnerabilities. WHERE: In the testing environment and staging areas.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the secure coding practices that must be followed during the application development process. It includes guidelines for input validation, error handling, and secure data storage to mitigate common vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement secure coding standards and conduct code reviews. WHO: Development teams and security officers. WHEN: During the development phase and prior to code deployment. HOW: Utilize automated tools for code analysis and conduct peer reviews. WHERE: All development environments and repositories.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates regular vulnerability testing for all applications to identify and remediate security weaknesses. Testing must be conducted both pre-deployment and periodically post-deployment.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct vulnerability assessments and penetration testing. WHO: Security testing teams and third-party security consultants. WHEN: Before application deployment and every six months thereafter. HOW: Use industry-standard testing tools and methodologies. WHERE: All production and staging environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes requirements for timely application of security patches and updates to ensure ongoing protection against vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Monitor and apply security patches as they are released. WHO: IT operations and application support teams. WHEN: Within 48 hours of patch release for critical vulnerabilities. HOW: Utilize patch management tools to automate the process. WHERE: All application environments, including production and development.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting threat modeling and risk assessments during the system design phase. It mandates that all new systems undergo a thorough analysis to identify potential threats and vulnerabilities, and to evaluate the risks associated with them.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct threat modeling sessions and risk assessments for all new systems. WHO: System architects and security teams are responsible for conducting these assessments. WHEN: Assessments must be completed prior to system design approval. HOW: Utilize standardized threat modeling frameworks and risk assessment methodologies. WHERE: Applicable to all system development projects within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the integration of security controls into the system development lifecycle. It requires that security measures are identified, implemented, and tested at each phase of the system lifecycle.
