subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Identify and implement security controls at each phase of the system lifecycle. WHO: Development teams and security officers are responsible for ensuring compliance. WHEN: Security controls must be integrated during the design, implementation, and testing phases. HOW: Follow the organization's security control framework and guidelines. WHERE: Applies to all systems developed or maintained by the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates that all software developers receive training on secure coding practices at least once a year. The training will cover the latest secure coding techniques, common vulnerabilities, and mitigation strategies.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct secure coding training sessions. WHO: Training Coordinator and Development Team Leads. WHEN: Annually, with additional sessions as needed. HOW: Utilize online training modules and in-person workshops, followed by assessments to measure understanding. WHERE: Training will be conducted in the organization's training facilities and online platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires that all code undergoes a formal review process to identify and rectify security vulnerabilities before deployment. Code reviews must be documented and include input from at least one security expert.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a code review process for all software projects. WHO: Development Teams and Security Analysts. WHEN: Before deployment of any software release. HOW: Use automated tools for static code analysis followed by manual reviews. WHERE: Code reviews will take place in the development environment and must be documented in the project repository.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for implementing security testing during the development and acceptance phases. It includes guidelines for selecting appropriate testing methods, tools, and techniques to ensure comprehensive coverage of potential vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct security testing using automated tools and manual techniques at each development phase. WHO: Development teams and security analysts are responsible for executing the tests. WHEN: Security testing must be performed at the end of each development sprint and prior to project acceptance. HOW: Utilize industry-standard testing frameworks and tools to identify vulnerabilities, and document findings in a centralized repository. WHERE: This control applies to all development environments, including on-premises and cloud-based systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for assessing and selecting vendors for outsourced development projects. It includes criteria for evaluating vendor capabilities, security practices, and compliance with relevant standards.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a comprehensive assessment of potential vendors, including security practices and compliance with ISO/IEC 27002. WHO: The procurement team, in collaboration with the IT security team. WHEN: Prior to vendor selection and at least annually thereafter. HOW: Utilize a standardized vendor assessment checklist and scoring system to evaluate vendors. WHERE: Applicable to all vendors considered for outsourced development projects.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the monitoring and compliance requirements for outsourced development activities, ensuring that vendors adhere to contractual obligations and security standards.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a monitoring program to review vendor compliance with security standards and contractual obligations. WHO: The compliance team, with support from project managers. WHEN: Quarterly reviews and upon completion of each project phase. HOW: Conduct audits and assessments, and review vendor reports. WHERE: Applies to all outsourced development projects and associated vendors.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the access control measures for development staff to ensure they can only access operational credentials under strict conditions. It includes guidelines for temporary authorization and revocation processes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Development staff may access operational passwords or authentication tokens only when necessary for system support. WHO: Access is granted to authorized development personnel only. WHEN: Access must be granted on a temporary basis, with a defined start and end period. HOW: Access requests must be documented and approved by a designated supervisor, with a log maintained for auditing purposes. WHERE: This control applies to all development environments interacting with production systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for submitting change requests, including necessary documentation and approval processes. All change requests must be submitted through the designated change management system and include a detailed description of the change, its purpose, and potential impacts.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Submit a formal change request through the change management system. WHO: All personnel involved in change processes. WHEN: Change requests must be submitted at least two weeks prior to the planned implementation date. HOW: Utilize the change management system to document and track requests. WHERE: Applicable to all organizational units and systems involved in change management.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the process for assessing the impact of proposed changes on existing systems and processes. It requires a risk assessment to be conducted for each change request to identify potential risks and mitigation strategies.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a risk assessment for each change request. WHO: Change management team and relevant stakeholders. WHEN: Assessments must be completed within five business days of request submission. HOW: Use established risk assessment criteria and tools to evaluate impacts. WHERE: Applies to all change requests submitted through the change management system.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the approval process for change requests, including roles and responsibilities of approvers and the criteria for approval. All changes must receive formal approval before implementation.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Review and approve change requests based on impact assessments. WHO: Change advisory board and designated approvers. WHEN: Approval must be granted at least one week prior to implementation. HOW: Utilize the change management system for tracking approvals. WHERE: Applies to all organizational units involved in the change management process.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the access control measures required to protect test information from unauthorized access. It specifies the roles and responsibilities for granting, reviewing, and revoking access to test data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement role-based access controls to restrict access to test information. WHO: Access control will be managed by the IT Security team in collaboration with department heads. WHEN: Access reviews will be conducted quarterly, and access will be revoked immediately upon personnel changes. HOW: Access will be managed through an identity and access management system with logging capabilities. WHERE: This control applies to all systems and applications that store or process test information.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Data Protection Measures for Test Information
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the data protection measures that must be implemented to safeguard test information, including encryption and data masking techniques.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All test information must be encrypted at rest and in transit. WHO: The IT department is responsible for implementing encryption protocols. WHEN: Encryption must be applied during the initial setup of test environments and reviewed annually. HOW: Encryption will be enforced using industry-standard algorithms and tools. WHERE: This applies to all storage locations and transmission channels for test data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the access control measures that must be implemented during audit testing to protect information systems. It specifies the roles and responsibilities of personnel involved in the audit process and the procedures for granting and revoking access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement role-based access controls to restrict access to information systems during audit testing. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for managing access controls, while audit team leaders are responsible for requesting access. WHEN: Access must be granted prior to the start of the audit and revoked immediately after completion. HOW: Access requests must be documented and approved by the IT Security Team, with periodic reviews conducted to ensure compliance. WHERE: This control applies to all information systems involved in audit testing across all organizational units.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for users to authenticate telecommunications carriers effectively. It includes guidelines for using encryption technologies and recognizing legitimate service providers.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Users must utilize provided authentication methods to verify telecommunications carriers. WHO: All users of telecommunications services. WHEN: Authentication must occur before accessing any telecommunications service. HOW: Users will be trained on the use of encryption technologies and identification methods. WHERE: This applies to all telecommunications access points, including remote and mobile environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for the installation and maintenance of intruder detection systems at telecommunications operations centers. It specifies the types of systems to be used, installation locations, and maintenance schedules to ensure effectiveness.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Install and maintain adequate physical intruder detection systems at all telecommunications operations centers. WHO: Security management team is responsible for overseeing the installation and maintenance. WHEN: Systems must be installed prior to facility operation and maintained quarterly. HOW: Use industry-standard detection systems and conduct regular testing to ensure functionality. WHERE: All telecommunications operations centers and associated facilities.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for the installation and maintenance of physical barriers around telecommunications facilities. It includes guidelines for barrier types, locations, and enforcement of local security policies.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Install effective physical barriers around all telecommunications facilities. WHO: Facilities management team is responsible for installation and enforcement. WHEN: Barriers must be installed before facility operation and inspected bi-annually. HOW: Follow best practices for barrier installation and conduct regular inspections. WHERE: All telecommunications facilities, including data centers and switching facilities.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for responding to violations of physical security policies, including malfunctioning barriers and detection systems. It specifies the roles and responsibilities for addressing these issues.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Immediate resolution of any physical security policy violations or malfunctions. WHO: Management at the appropriate level is responsible for addressing issues. WHEN: Issues must be resolved within 24 hours of identification. HOW: Implement a reporting system for violations and conduct follow-up investigations. WHERE: All telecommunications facilities and operations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for managing visitor data, including recording, storing, and protecting visitor information. It specifies the responsibilities of personnel involved in visitor management and the security measures required to safeguard this data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Visitor data must be recorded upon entry, including name, purpose of visit, and time of entry. WHO: The reception staff are responsible for recording and managing visitor data. WHEN: Visitor data must be recorded at the time of entry and updated as necessary during the visit. HOW: Data must be recorded in a secure visitor management system that encrypts information and restricts access to authorized personnel only. WHERE: This control applies to all telecommunication operation rooms and control centers.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific access control measures that must be implemented to secure offices, rooms, and facilities. It includes guidelines for the use of identification badges, electronic access systems, and visitor logs.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement identification badges for all personnel and electronic access systems for sensitive areas. WHO: Security personnel and facility management teams are responsible for implementation. WHEN: Access control measures must be established prior to occupancy and reviewed annually. HOW: Access logs will be maintained and reviewed monthly to identify unauthorized access attempts. WHERE: All organizational offices, rooms, and facilities must adhere to these access control measures.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for surveillance and monitoring of organizational facilities to deter unauthorized access and ensure safety.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Install surveillance cameras in strategic locations and monitor access points. WHO: IT security team and facility management are responsible for installation and monitoring. WHEN: Surveillance systems must be operational before facility access is granted and reviewed quarterly. HOW: Recorded footage will be stored for a minimum of 30 days and reviewed in case of security incidents. WHERE: Surveillance must cover all entrances, exits, and sensitive areas within organizational facilities.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for the installation and management of surveillance systems to monitor physical security effectively. It includes guidelines for camera placement, data retention, and access to surveillance footage.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Install surveillance cameras in strategic locations to monitor access points and sensitive areas. WHO: Security personnel and IT department are responsible for installation and maintenance. WHEN: Installation must be completed within 3 months of policy approval, with quarterly reviews of camera effectiveness. HOW: Use industry-standard surveillance equipment and software for monitoring and data storage. WHERE: All organizational premises, including offices and data centers.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the procedures for managing access controls to ensure that only authorized personnel can enter secure areas. It includes guidelines for issuing access badges and monitoring access logs.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a badge access system for all secure areas, with unique identifiers for each employee. WHO: HR and security personnel are responsible for issuing badges and monitoring access. WHEN: Access badges must be issued within 1 week of employment, with monthly audits of access logs. HOW: Utilize access control software to track entries and exits. WHERE: All secure areas within organizational premises.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for securing organizational facilities against unauthorized access and environmental threats. It includes measures for access control, surveillance, and emergency preparedness.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement access control systems, surveillance cameras, and emergency response plans. WHO: Facility managers and security personnel are responsible for implementation. WHEN: Access control systems must be operational within 30 days, and emergency response plans should be reviewed annually. HOW: Use electronic access logs, conduct regular security drills, and maintain surveillance footage for 90 days. WHERE: Applies to all organizational facilities, including offices and data centers.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for monitoring environmental conditions and responding to potential threats such as floods, fires, and other natural disasters.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Install environmental monitoring systems and establish response protocols for environmental threats. WHO: IT and facilities management teams are responsible for monitoring and response. WHEN: Environmental monitoring systems must be installed within 60 days, and response protocols should be tested quarterly. HOW: Utilize automated alerts for environmental changes and conduct drills for response procedures. WHERE: Applies to all organizational facilities and remote work environments.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Access Control Procedures for Secure Areas
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for granting, monitoring, and revoking access to secure areas. It includes the steps for verifying identity, logging access, and conducting audits to ensure compliance with the Secure Area Access Policy.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement access control procedures for secure areas, including identity verification and access logging. WHO: Security personnel and IT administrators are responsible for executing these procedures. WHEN: Access verification must occur prior to entry, and logs must be reviewed weekly. HOW: Utilize access control systems and surveillance cameras to monitor entry points. WHERE: Applicable to all secure areas within the organization, including data centers and server rooms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for maintaining a clear desk and screen policy, including guidelines for document handling, screen locking, and workspace organization.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Employees must clear their desks of all sensitive documents and lock their screens when leaving their workspace. WHO: All employees, contractors, and third-party personnel. WHEN: At the end of each workday and whenever leaving the workspace for any duration. HOW: Employees will use password protection for screens and secure storage for documents. WHERE: Applies to all organizational locations, including offices, remote workspaces, and third-party sites.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific requirements for protecting telecommunications equipment located in shared data centers. It mandates the implementation of additional security measures to safeguard customer information stored in these systems.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement additional security measures for telecommunications equipment, including physical barriers and access controls. WHO: Telecommunications security teams and facilities management personnel are responsible for implementation. WHEN: Security measures must be in place prior to the installation of telecommunications equipment and reviewed annually. HOW: Use security assessments and audits to evaluate the effectiveness of implemented measures. WHERE: This subpolicy applies to all telecommunications equipment located in shared data centers and other relevant facilities.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific procedures for handling organizational assets when taken off-premises, including transportation, storage, and usage guidelines to ensure security.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Employees must use secure transport methods for assets, store them in locked containers, and utilize encryption for sensitive data. WHO: All employees and contractors handling organizational assets off-premises. WHEN: Procedures must be followed at all times when assets are off-premises. HOW: Compliance will be monitored through regular audits and employee training sessions. WHERE: This applies to all locations where organizational assets are taken off-premises, including client sites, public spaces, and remote work environments.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Secure Storage and Access Control for Storage Media
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for the secure storage and access control of all storage media within the organization. It mandates that all storage media must be stored in secure locations and that access is restricted to authorized personnel only.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement secure storage solutions for all storage media and restrict access to authorized personnel. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for implementing and monitoring access controls. WHEN: Access controls must be reviewed quarterly, and any unauthorized access must be reported immediately. HOW: Use access control systems and physical security measures to restrict access. WHERE: All locations where storage media is stored, including on-premises and cloud environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the procedures for the secure disposal of storage media to prevent data leakage. It includes guidelines for physical destruction and data wiping methods.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All storage media must be securely wiped or physically destroyed before disposal. WHO: IT Department is responsible for ensuring compliance with disposal procedures. WHEN: Disposal must occur immediately after the media is no longer needed or at the end of its lifecycle. HOW: Use certified data destruction services or approved data wiping software. WHERE: Disposal must occur at designated disposal sites or through certified vendors.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for the installation and maintenance of uninterruptible power supply systems in telecommunications facilities. It includes specifications for capacity, maintenance schedules, and testing procedures.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Install UPS systems with sufficient capacity for all critical loads. WHO: Facility management team and electrical engineers. WHEN: Installation must be completed within 6 months of policy approval, with quarterly maintenance checks thereafter. HOW: Use industry-standard UPS systems and conduct load testing every quarter. WHERE: All telecommunications facilities operated by the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the management of emergency power generators, including fuel storage, maintenance, and operational readiness.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Ensure emergency power generators are operational and have adequate fuel reserves. WHO: Facility management team and safety officers. WHEN: Fuel checks must be conducted monthly, with a full operational test of generators every 6 months. HOW: Maintain a log of fuel levels and generator tests, ensuring compliance with safety regulations. WHERE: All telecommunications facilities and associated storage locations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for maintaining HVAC systems in telecommunications facilities to ensure optimal operating conditions for equipment.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct regular maintenance and inspections of HVAC systems. WHO: Facility management team and HVAC technicians. WHEN: Maintenance must occur bi-annually, with inspections conducted monthly. HOW: Use a checklist for maintenance tasks and document all findings and actions taken. WHERE: All equipment rooms within telecommunications facilities.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for monitoring telecommunications cabling and conducting regular audits to ensure compliance with the Cabling Security Policy. It includes guidelines for the installation of monitoring systems and the frequency of audits.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement active monitoring systems for telecommunications cabling and conduct regular audits of access points. WHO: IT security team is responsible for monitoring and auditing. WHEN: Monitoring should be continuous, with audits conducted quarterly. HOW: Use specialized monitoring tools to detect unauthorized access and maintain audit logs for review. WHERE: Applies to all locations where telecommunications cabling is installed, including on-premises and remote sites.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for scheduling and conducting regular maintenance on all organizational equipment. It includes guidelines for frequency, responsible personnel, and documentation requirements.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish a maintenance schedule for all equipment based on manufacturer recommendations and operational needs. WHO: The Facilities Management team is responsible for creating and updating the maintenance schedule. WHEN: Maintenance schedules must be reviewed and updated quarterly. HOW: Utilize a maintenance management system to track scheduled and completed maintenance activities. WHERE: This control applies to all organizational locations where equipment is utilized.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the protocol for addressing emergency maintenance needs that arise unexpectedly. It includes escalation procedures and communication guidelines.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement an emergency maintenance protocol that allows for immediate response to equipment failures. WHO: The IT Support team is responsible for executing emergency maintenance procedures. WHEN: Emergency maintenance must be initiated within 1 hour of identifying a critical equipment failure. HOW: Use a ticketing system to log emergency maintenance requests and track resolution. WHERE: This protocol applies to all critical equipment across all operational environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for data sanitization of equipment prior to disposal or re-use. It includes methods for data wiping, degaussing, and physical destruction of storage media.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement data sanitization methods such as data wiping, degaussing, or physical destruction. WHO: IT department is responsible for executing data sanitization. WHEN: Data sanitization must occur prior to any disposal or re-use of equipment. HOW: Use approved data sanitization tools and techniques as per ISO/IEC 27002 standards. WHERE: All organizational facilities where equipment is processed for disposal or re-use.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the creation and maintenance of documentation for all equipment disposal and re-use activities, ensuring an audit trail is available for compliance verification.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Maintain records of all disposal and re-use activities, including equipment details and sanitization methods used. WHO: Compliance officer is responsible for overseeing documentation. WHEN: Documentation must be completed immediately after disposal or re-use actions. HOW: Use a centralized tracking system to log all disposal activities. WHERE: Documentation must be stored securely within the organization's compliance management system.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for access control measures to be implemented at communication centres. It includes guidelines for personnel identification, visitor management, and access logging.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement access control systems including keycard entry and biometric scanners. WHO: Security personnel and IT department are responsible for managing access controls. WHEN: Access controls must be reviewed and updated quarterly. HOW: Access logs will be maintained and monitored for unauthorized access attempts. WHERE: This applies to all entry points of communication centres.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for surveillance systems to monitor communication centres. It includes the installation of CCTV cameras and monitoring protocols.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Install CCTV cameras at all entry and exit points, as well as within critical areas of communication centres. WHO: Facilities management is responsible for installation and maintenance. WHEN: Surveillance systems must be operational 24/7. HOW: Recorded footage will be reviewed weekly for any suspicious activity. WHERE: This applies to all communication centres operated by the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the environmental controls necessary to protect communication centres from physical threats such as fire, flooding, and power outages.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement fire suppression systems, flood barriers, and uninterruptible power supplies (UPS). WHO: Facilities management is responsible for the implementation and maintenance of these systems. WHEN: Environmental controls must be tested bi-annually. HOW: Regular drills and inspections will be conducted to ensure functionality. WHERE: This applies to all communication centres.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Access Control for Telecommunications Equipment Rooms
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for implementing access control measures to restrict entry to telecommunications equipment rooms. It includes guidelines for the use of identification systems, visitor management, and access logs to ensure that only authorized personnel can access these critical areas.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement access control systems that require identification for entry. WHO: Security personnel and IT staff are responsible for managing access control. WHEN: Access controls must be reviewed and updated quarterly or as personnel changes occur. HOW: Use electronic access control systems and maintain logs of all entries and exits. WHERE: This control applies to all telecommunications equipment rooms across the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the environmental security measures necessary to protect telecommunications equipment rooms from fire, flooding, and other environmental hazards. It includes guidelines for fire suppression systems, temperature and humidity controls, and regular maintenance checks.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Install fire suppression systems and environmental monitoring equipment. WHO: Facilities management is responsible for the installation and maintenance of these systems. WHEN: Environmental controls must be inspected bi-annually. HOW: Conduct regular maintenance checks and testing of fire suppression systems. WHERE: This control applies to all telecommunications equipment rooms within the organization.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Physical Security Controls for Isolated Areas
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific physical security controls that must be implemented in isolated operating areas to protect telecommunications facilities. It includes requirements for structural integrity, access control, and environmental protections.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement physical security measures including earthquake-proofing, access control systems, and surveillance equipment. WHO: Security personnel and facility management teams are responsible for the implementation. WHEN: Controls must be implemented prior to the operation of any telecommunications facility in isolated areas and reviewed annually. HOW: Use industry standards and best practices for physical security design and conduct regular audits to ensure compliance. WHERE: This applies to all isolated operating areas designated for telecommunications operations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for the installation of telecommunications equipment in other carriers' premises, focusing on isolation and security measures to protect the equipment from unauthorized access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Ensure that all equipment is installed in a designated secure area with clear boundaries defined. WHO: Installation teams and security management personnel are responsible for implementing these procedures. WHEN: Procedures must be followed during the installation phase and reviewed annually. HOW: Use checklists to verify compliance with isolation and security measures, and conduct audits to assess adherence. WHERE: Applicable to all installations in third-party carrier locations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for establishing and managing agreements with other telecommunications organizations for the provision of support utilities necessary for equipment operation.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish formal agreements for support utilities with other carriers. WHO: Legal and operational management teams are responsible for drafting and reviewing agreements. WHEN: Agreements must be established prior to equipment installation and reviewed bi-annually. HOW: Utilize a standardized agreement template and maintain records of all agreements. WHERE: Relevant to all third-party carrier locations where equipment is installed.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the verification of security levels at the premises of other carriers to ensure they meet the organization's security standards.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct security assessments of third-party carrier premises before equipment installation. WHO: Security assessment teams are responsible for conducting evaluations. WHEN: Assessments must be completed at least 30 days prior to installation. HOW: Use a standardized security assessment checklist and document findings. WHERE: Applicable to all third-party carrier locations where equipment is to be installed.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific physical security measures that must be implemented to protect telecommunications equipment located at customer premises. It includes guidelines for equipment installation, monitoring, and access control.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement physical security measures including sturdy installation, access controls, and monitoring systems. WHO: Security personnel and installation teams are responsible for implementing these measures. WHEN: Security measures must be in place prior to equipment installation and continuously monitored thereafter. HOW: Use physical barriers, surveillance systems, and access logs to monitor and control access. WHERE: This applies to all customer premises where telecommunications equipment is installed.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the protocols for detecting unauthorized modifications to telecommunications equipment at customer premises. It includes guidelines for regular inspections and monitoring.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish protocols for regular inspections and monitoring of equipment to detect unauthorized modifications. WHO: Maintenance teams and security personnel are responsible for conducting inspections. WHEN: Inspections must be conducted quarterly or upon any suspected unauthorized access. HOW: Utilize monitoring tools and logs to track changes and modifications. WHERE: This applies to all locations where telecommunications equipment is installed at customer premises.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the requirements for managing the boundary and interface between telecommunications equipment and customer equipment. It includes guidelines for isolation and access control.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Define and manage the boundary and interface between telecommunications and customer equipment, ensuring clear isolation protocols. WHO: Network engineers and installation teams are responsible for boundary management. WHEN: This must be established during the initial installation and reviewed annually. HOW: Document and implement isolation procedures and access controls. WHERE: This applies to all customer premises where telecommunications equipment interfaces with customer systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for conducting background checks on employees and contractors who will have access to sensitive information or telecommunications equipment. It specifies the types of checks to be performed and the documentation required.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct background checks including criminal records, employment history, and educational verification. WHO: The HR department is responsible for initiating and overseeing the background check process. WHEN: Background checks must be completed prior to granting access to sensitive information or equipment. HOW: Utilize third-party services for background checks and maintain records of all checks performed. WHERE: Applicable to all personnel in telecommunications and critical national infrastructure roles.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific responsibilities of employees regarding the protection of personally identifiable information and other confidential information. It includes training requirements, reporting procedures for breaches, and guidelines for handling sensitive data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Employees must complete mandatory training on data protection and confidentiality. WHO: All employees engaged in telecommunications services. WHEN: Training must be completed within the first month of employment and annually thereafter. HOW: Training will be conducted through online modules and assessments. WHERE: Training is applicable to all organizational units involved in telecommunications services.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for employees to comply with non-disclosure agreements (NDAs) related to confidential information. It includes guidelines for signing, maintaining, and enforcing NDAs.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Employees must sign NDAs prior to accessing confidential information. WHO: All employees and contractors with access to sensitive data. WHEN: NDAs must be signed before the commencement of employment or contract. HOW: NDAs will be reviewed and signed electronically through the HR management system. WHERE: This applies to all locations where employees access confidential information.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for mandatory security awareness training for all personnel. It specifies the content, frequency, and delivery methods for the training program.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct mandatory security awareness training sessions. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for developing and delivering the training. WHEN: Training must be completed within the first month of employment and annually thereafter. HOW: Training will be delivered through online modules and in-person workshops, with assessments to measure understanding. WHERE: Training will be accessible via the organization's learning management system and conducted in designated training rooms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for conducting investigations into alleged policy violations. It includes guidelines for gathering evidence, interviewing witnesses, and documenting findings.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a thorough investigation of alleged violations by gathering evidence and interviewing relevant parties. WHO: The HR department is responsible for leading the investigation, with support from department managers as needed. WHEN: Investigations should commence within 48 hours of the reported violation and be completed within 14 days. HOW: Utilize standardized investigation forms and interview protocols to ensure consistency and thoroughness. WHERE: Investigations will take place in a confidential setting, ensuring privacy for all parties involved.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for documenting disciplinary actions and reporting outcomes to relevant stakeholders.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document all findings, actions taken, and outcomes of the disciplinary process. WHO: The HR department is responsible for maintaining records, with input from involved managers. WHEN: Documentation must be completed within 5 business days following the conclusion of the investigation. HOW: Use a centralized electronic system for record-keeping to ensure accessibility and security. WHERE: Documentation will be stored in the HR information system, accessible only to authorized personnel.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the enforcement of disciplinary actions determined by the investigation outcomes, including communication to the affected employee.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement disciplinary actions as determined by the investigation findings, including verbal or written warnings, suspension, or termination. WHO: The HR department, in collaboration with department managers, will enforce the actions. WHEN: Disciplinary actions must be communicated to the employee within 3 business days of the investigation conclusion. HOW: Conduct a formal meeting with the employee to discuss the findings and the resulting actions. WHERE: Meetings will be held in a private setting to ensure confidentiality.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for revoking access to organizational systems and data upon termination or change of employment. It includes steps for notifying IT and ensuring that all access rights are removed in a timely manner.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Access to all organizational systems and data must be revoked immediately upon termination or change of employment. WHO: The HR department will notify the IT department of the termination or change. WHEN: Access revocation must occur within 24 hours of notification. HOW: IT will use access management tools to remove user accounts and permissions. WHERE: This applies to all systems and applications used by the employee.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for the return of all organizational property, including physical assets and electronic devices, upon termination or change of employment.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All organizational property must be returned, including laptops, mobile devices, and access cards. WHO: The departing employee is responsible for returning all items to their manager or designated HR representative. WHEN: All items must be returned on or before the last working day. HOW: A checklist will be provided to ensure all items are accounted for. WHERE: Returns must be made at the employee's primary work location.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for drafting, reviewing, and signing confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements. It includes guidelines for determining the necessity of NDAs based on the type of information being shared and the parties involved.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Draft and review confidentiality agreements for all personnel and third-party vendors accessing sensitive information. WHO: The Legal Department is responsible for drafting and reviewing agreements, while department heads must ensure all personnel sign them. WHEN: Agreements must be signed before any access to sensitive information is granted. HOW: Use a standardized template for NDAs and maintain a log of signed agreements. WHERE: This applies to all departments and locations where sensitive information is handled.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the security controls required for employees accessing organizational resources remotely. It includes guidelines for secure connections, device security, and data handling practices to mitigate risks associated with remote work.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Employees must use a Virtual Private Network (VPN) to access organizational resources remotely. Devices used for remote work must have up-to-date antivirus software and firewalls enabled. Sensitive data must be encrypted during transmission. WHO: All employees working remotely are responsible for adhering to these controls. IT department will provide support and resources. WHEN: Controls must be implemented prior to remote work and maintained throughout the duration of remote access. HOW: Employees will receive training on secure remote access practices, and IT will conduct regular audits to ensure compliance. WHERE: These controls apply to all organizational systems accessed remotely, including cloud services and on-premises applications.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific procedures for reporting information security events, including the channels to be used, the information to be provided, and the timelines for reporting.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Employees must report any suspected information security events via the designated reporting tool. WHO: All employees, contractors, and third-party vendors are responsible for reporting. WHEN: Reports must be submitted within 24 hours of detection of the event. HOW: Reports should include details such as the nature of the event, affected systems, and any immediate actions taken. WHERE: Reporting must be done through the organization's secure incident reporting portal.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for granting, managing, and revoking access to information systems and data. It ensures that access is granted based on the principle of least privilege and that all access is logged and monitored.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) for all information systems. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for managing access rights. WHEN: Access reviews will be conducted quarterly, and access will be revoked immediately upon termination of employment. HOW: Access requests must be submitted through the organization's access management system, and all access logs will be reviewed during audits. WHERE: This control applies to all information systems and applications used within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the measures for protecting sensitive data throughout its lifecycle, including data classification, encryption, and secure disposal.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All sensitive data must be classified and encrypted at rest and in transit. WHO: Data Owners are responsible for classifying data, while IT Security is responsible for implementing encryption. WHEN: Data classification must occur upon creation, and encryption must be applied before data storage or transmission. HOW: Data classification guidelines will be provided, and encryption will be enforced using approved cryptographic methods. WHERE: This policy applies to all data stored on organizational servers, cloud services, and portable devices.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the process for defining and assigning information security roles within the organization. It includes criteria for role assignment, documentation requirements, and the process for updating roles as necessary.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Define and document specific information security roles and responsibilities. WHO: Information Security Officer (ISO) and HR Department. WHEN: Annually or as roles change. HOW: Utilize a role assignment template and review process to ensure clarity and accountability. WHERE: Applicable to all departments and personnel involved in information security.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates training and awareness programs for all personnel regarding their information security roles and responsibilities. It includes training frequency, content, and evaluation methods.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct training sessions on information security roles and responsibilities. WHO: Information Security Officer (ISO) and Training Department. WHEN: At onboarding and annually thereafter. HOW: Implement training modules and assessments to measure understanding. WHERE: All organizational locations and remote environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for defining and assigning roles to ensure proper segregation of duties. It mandates that all roles related to transaction processing must be documented and approved by management.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Define and document roles related to transaction processing. WHO: Department heads are responsible for role definition and assignment. WHEN: Roles must be reviewed and updated annually or upon significant organizational changes. HOW: Utilize a role management tool to document and track role assignments. WHERE: This applies to all departments involved in financial and operational transactions.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the process for approving transactions to ensure that no individual can initiate and approve their own transactions. It includes guidelines for multi-level approvals based on transaction value.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a multi-level approval process for transactions. WHO: All employees involved in transaction processing must adhere to this process. WHEN: This process must be followed for every transaction initiated. HOW: Use an automated workflow system to enforce approval hierarchies. WHERE: This applies to all financial and operational transactions across the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the specific responsibilities of management in overseeing compliance with security standards and ensuring effective governance practices are in place. It outlines the processes for regular reviews and updates of security policies and practices.
subpolicies.Control: Management shall conduct quarterly reviews of security policies and practices to ensure compliance with ISO/IEC 27002. The Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) is responsible for coordinating these reviews (WHO). Reviews will include assessments of current policies, identification of gaps, and recommendations for improvements (WHAT). Reviews will be conducted quarterly, with reports submitted to the executive team within two weeks of completion (WHEN). The review process will utilize established assessment criteria and tools to measure compliance and effectiveness (HOW). This control applies to all management personnel and relevant stakeholders involved in security governance (WHERE).

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for verifying the legitimacy of inquiries from law enforcement and investigative agencies before disclosing any information. It includes steps for documentation and communication with relevant authorities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Verify the legitimacy of all inquiries from law enforcement before disclosing information. WHO: Designated compliance officers and legal counsel. WHEN: Each time an inquiry is received. HOW: Utilize established verification procedures, including checking the authority's credentials and the legal basis for the request. WHERE: Applicable to all telecommunications systems and personnel involved in handling such inquiries.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates training for all relevant personnel on the procedures for handling inquiries from authorities, ensuring they understand the legal implications and organizational responsibilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct training sessions on handling inquiries from law enforcement. WHO: All personnel involved in compliance and customer service. WHEN: Annually and upon onboarding of new employees. HOW: Implement training programs and assessments to ensure understanding of procedures. WHERE: Training to be conducted in all operational locations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the documentation of all engagements with special interest groups, including the purpose, participants, and outcomes of each interaction. It requires that records be maintained in a centralized system accessible for review and compliance purposes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document all engagements with special interest groups, including meeting notes and correspondence. WHO: All employees involved in such engagements. WHEN: Documentation must be completed within 48 hours of the engagement. HOW: Use the designated documentation system to record details. WHERE: All organizational units engaging with special interest groups must adhere to this requirement.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Compliance with Legal and Ethical Standards
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy ensures that all interactions with special interest groups comply with applicable laws and ethical standards. It requires training for employees on relevant regulations and organizational policies regarding engagement.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct training sessions on legal and ethical standards for engaging with special interest groups. WHO: Compliance department to oversee training; all employees engaging with these groups must attend. WHEN: Training must be conducted annually or as needed. HOW: Utilize internal training resources and external experts as necessary. WHERE: Training sessions will be held in designated training facilities or online platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for collecting threat intelligence data from various sources, including open-source intelligence, commercial threat feeds, and internal security logs. It specifies the roles responsible for data collection and the methods to be employed.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Collect threat intelligence data from identified sources. WHO: Security Operations Team and Threat Intelligence Analysts. WHEN: Data collection shall occur on a daily basis or as new threats are identified. HOW: Utilize automated tools and manual processes to gather data from threat feeds and internal logs. WHERE: Data collection will occur across all organizational systems and external threat intelligence platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the processes for analyzing collected threat intelligence and generating reports for stakeholders. It includes guidelines for assessing the relevance and credibility of threat data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Analyze collected threat intelligence and prepare reports. WHO: Threat Intelligence Analysts and Security Management. WHEN: Analysis shall be conducted weekly, with immediate reporting for critical threats. HOW: Use established analytical frameworks and tools to assess threat data. WHERE: Analysis will be performed in the Security Operations Center and shared with relevant stakeholders via secure communication channels.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting security risk assessments for all projects. It specifies the process for identifying, analyzing, and mitigating security risks throughout the project lifecycle.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a security risk assessment at the initiation phase of each project and at key milestones. WHO: Project managers and security officers are responsible for executing the assessments. WHEN: Assessments must be conducted at project initiation and every three months thereafter or when significant changes occur. HOW: Use standardized risk assessment tools and methodologies to identify and evaluate risks. WHERE: Applicable to all projects across all organizational units and locations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates adherence to ISO/IEC 27002 guidelines in all project management activities. It provides a framework for ensuring that security controls are implemented effectively.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Ensure that all project documentation and processes align with ISO/IEC 27002 standards. WHO: Project managers and compliance officers are responsible for monitoring adherence. WHEN: Compliance checks must be performed at project initiation and at each project milestone. HOW: Conduct regular audits and reviews of project documentation against ISO/IEC 27002 requirements. WHERE: Applies to all projects and related documentation within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific requirements for developing and maintaining the inventory of telecommunications assets. It includes guidelines for documenting asset details, defining responsibilities, and ensuring comprehensive coverage of all relevant assets.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and maintain a comprehensive inventory of telecommunications assets, including hardware, software, and services. WHO: The IT Asset Management team is responsible for maintaining the inventory, while department heads must ensure their teams provide accurate asset information. WHEN: The inventory must be reviewed and updated at least annually or whenever significant changes occur. HOW: Utilize asset management software to track and document assets, ensuring all entries are verified for accuracy. WHERE: This control applies to all organizational units involved in telecommunications and information management.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific responsibilities of users regarding the acceptable use of information and associated assets. It provides detailed guidelines on how users should interact with organizational resources to ensure compliance with the overarching policy.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Users must adhere to the guidelines for acceptable use of information and assets. WHO: All employees, contractors, and third-party vendors. WHEN: At all times during their engagement with the organization. HOW: Users will receive training on acceptable use practices and must acknowledge understanding through a signed agreement. WHERE: This applies to all organizational systems, applications, and devices used for accessing information.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the step-by-step process for returning organizational assets, including the roles responsible for overseeing the return process and the documentation required for verification.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Employees must complete an Asset Return Form and submit it to their manager. All assets must be returned to the designated asset manager. WHO: Employees, asset managers, and HR personnel are responsible for ensuring compliance. WHEN: Asset returns must occur within 5 business days of termination or contract completion. HOW: The Asset Return Form must be filled out accurately, and a physical inventory check must be conducted. WHERE: The return process applies to all organizational locations and remote work environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific guidelines for classifying information based on sensitivity and criticality. It provides detailed criteria for determining classification levels and the handling requirements for each level.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Classify all information into predefined categories (e.g., Public, Internal, Confidential, and Restricted). WHO: All employees and contractors involved in information management. WHEN: Classification must occur at the point of creation or acquisition of information and reviewed annually. HOW: Utilize the classification framework and tools provided by the organization to assess and categorize information. WHERE: Applicable to all information systems and environments where organizational data is processed or stored.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific procedures for classifying and labelling information assets based on their sensitivity and required handling protocols. It includes guidelines for determining classification levels and the appropriate labelling formats.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement classification and labelling procedures for all information assets. WHO: Information Security Officer and Department Heads are responsible for overseeing compliance. WHEN: Procedures must be reviewed and updated annually or as needed. HOW: Use a standardized classification framework and labelling tools to ensure consistency. WHERE: Applies to all information systems, storage locations, and personnel involved in information management.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for securely transferring information via email, including encryption standards and recipient verification processes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All emails containing sensitive information must be encrypted using AES-256 encryption. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for implementing encryption protocols. WHEN: Encryption must be applied to all outgoing emails containing sensitive data before sending. HOW: Utilize approved email encryption tools and verify recipient email addresses against the organization's directory. WHERE: This control applies to all email communications originating from organizational email accounts.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy provides guidelines for securely transferring files, including the use of secure file transfer protocols and access controls.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All file transfers must utilize secure protocols such as SFTP or HTTPS. WHO: Employees responsible for file transfers must adhere to these guidelines. WHEN: This requirement is applicable to all file transfers conducted during business operations. HOW: Implement secure file transfer solutions and ensure that access controls are enforced to limit file access to authorized personnel only. WHERE: This control applies to all systems and applications used for file transfers within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for implementing role-based access controls within the organization. It specifies the process for defining user roles, assigning permissions, and managing access rights based on job functions.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement role-based access controls by defining user roles and assigning permissions accordingly. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for defining roles and managing access permissions. WHEN: Role definitions and access permissions must be reviewed and updated quarterly or as job functions change. HOW: Access permissions will be managed through an access control management system that logs all changes and access requests. WHERE: This control applies to all organizational systems and applications that handle sensitive telecommunications data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for monitoring user access and conducting regular reviews to ensure compliance with access control policies.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Monitor user access to all organizational resources and conduct regular reviews of access logs. WHO: The Compliance Team is responsible for monitoring access and conducting reviews. WHEN: Access logs must be reviewed monthly, and any discrepancies must be addressed immediately. HOW: Access monitoring will be conducted using automated tools that generate reports on user access patterns and anomalies. WHERE: This control applies to all systems and applications used for telecommunications services.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for granting access to users based on their roles and responsibilities. It includes the steps for requesting, approving, and provisioning access rights to systems and applications.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a standardized process for user access requests and approvals. WHO: The IT Security team is responsible for managing access requests, while department heads must approve access based on role requirements. WHEN: Access requests must be processed within 48 hours of submission. HOW: Utilize an access management system to track requests and approvals. WHERE: This control applies to all organizational systems and applications requiring user authentication.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates regular reviews of user access rights to ensure compliance with the principle of least privilege and to identify any unauthorized access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct bi-annual reviews of user access rights across all systems. WHO: The IT Security team will perform the reviews, with input from department heads. WHEN: Reviews must be completed every six months, with findings reported to senior management. HOW: Use automated tools to generate access reports for review. WHERE: This control applies to all systems and applications where user access is granted.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the process for revoking access rights when an employee leaves the organization or changes roles.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a formal process for revoking access immediately upon termination or role change. WHO: HR is responsible for notifying the IT Security team of any personnel changes. WHEN: Access must be revoked within 24 hours of notification. HOW: Utilize the access management system to ensure timely revocation. WHERE: This control applies to all organizational systems and applications.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) across all critical systems to enhance security and protect against unauthorized access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement multi-factor authentication for all critical systems. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for implementation and monitoring. WHEN: MFA must be deployed within 90 days of policy approval and reviewed annually. HOW: Utilize industry-standard MFA solutions and conduct regular testing to ensure effectiveness. WHERE: Applicable to all organizational systems classified as critical, including cloud services and on-premises applications.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy provides guidelines for creating, managing, and storing passwords securely to prevent unauthorized access to systems and data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish guidelines for password complexity, expiration, and storage. WHO: All employees and contractors must comply with these guidelines. WHEN: Passwords must be updated every 90 days, and new accounts must follow the guidelines immediately upon creation. HOW: Use password management tools to store and generate secure passwords. WHERE: Applies to all systems and applications requiring user authentication.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedure for requesting, approving, and documenting user access to organizational systems and data. It specifies the roles involved in the access request process and the documentation required for compliance.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Users must submit access requests through the designated access management system. WHO: Access requests must be approved by the user's manager and the IT security officer. WHEN: Requests must be submitted at least 5 business days prior to the required access date. HOW: Access requests will be tracked in the access management system, which will generate reports for compliance audits. WHERE: This procedure applies to all organizational systems and data requiring user access.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the process for conducting quarterly reviews of user access rights to ensure compliance with the Access Rights Management Policy. It defines the roles responsible for conducting the reviews and the criteria for evaluating access rights.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Quarterly reviews of user access rights must be conducted to verify compliance with the least privilege principle. WHO: The IT security team is responsible for conducting the reviews, with input from department heads. WHEN: Reviews must be completed within the first week of each quarter. HOW: Review findings will be documented and reported to senior management, with corrective actions taken for any discrepancies. WHERE: This procedure applies to all systems and data covered by the Access Rights Management Policy.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting security assessments of suppliers who have access to sensitive information. It includes criteria for evaluating the supplier's security capabilities and compliance with relevant standards.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a security assessment of each supplier prior to granting access to sensitive information. WHO: The procurement and IT security teams are responsible for conducting the assessments. WHEN: Assessments must be completed before the contract is signed and reviewed annually thereafter. HOW: Use a standardized assessment checklist based on ISO/IEC 27002 guidelines to evaluate the supplier's security measures. WHERE: Assessments apply to all suppliers with access to sensitive information across all organizational units.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the inclusion of confidentiality and non-disclosure agreements in contracts with suppliers who handle sensitive information.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Ensure that all contracts with suppliers include confidentiality and non-disclosure agreements that specify the handling of sensitive information. WHO: The legal department is responsible for drafting and reviewing these agreements. WHEN: Agreements must be finalized before any sensitive information is shared with the supplier. HOW: Utilize a standard template for confidentiality agreements that aligns with ISO/IEC 27002 requirements. WHERE: This applies to all supplier contracts across all organizational units.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the process for monitoring compliance with legal and regulatory requirements related to supplier relationships.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a monitoring process to ensure compliance with all legal and regulatory requirements regarding supplier access to sensitive information. WHO: The compliance team is responsible for monitoring and reporting on compliance status. WHEN: Compliance reviews must be conducted quarterly and after any significant regulatory changes. HOW: Use compliance checklists and audits to assess adherence to legal requirements. WHERE: This applies to all suppliers with access to sensitive information across all organizational units.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific security clauses that must be included in all supplier agreements to ensure compliance with organizational security standards. It details the requirements for protection against service impairment, responsibility demarcation, and service availability.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Include clauses in supplier agreements that specify security requirements. WHO: Procurement and legal departments are responsible for drafting and reviewing agreements. WHEN: These clauses must be included in all new supplier agreements and reviewed annually for existing agreements. HOW: Use a standardized template for supplier agreements that incorporates these security clauses. WHERE: This applies to all supplier agreements across all organizational units engaging with third-party telecommunications services.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific controls that must be included in supplier agreements to protect sensitive customer data and ensure compliance with information security standards. It details the requirements for non-disclosure agreements, subcontracting restrictions, and the maintenance of data integrity throughout the supply chain.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish and enforce supplier agreements that include non-disclosure clauses, subcontracting restrictions, and data integrity requirements. WHO: The procurement department is responsible for drafting and negotiating supplier agreements, while the compliance team will review them for adherence to security standards. WHEN: Supplier agreements must be reviewed and updated annually or upon any significant change in supplier relationships. HOW: Use standardized templates for supplier agreements that incorporate security controls and conduct regular audits to ensure compliance. WHERE: This control applies to all supplier agreements related to telecommunications services and must be documented in the organization's contract management system.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for monitoring supplier performance against established service level agreements (SLAs) and key performance indicators (KPIs). It includes procedures for regular performance reviews and reporting mechanisms.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct performance reviews of suppliers against SLAs and KPIs. WHO: Procurement and compliance teams are responsible for monitoring. WHEN: Reviews will be conducted quarterly. HOW: Utilize performance metrics and reporting tools to assess compliance. WHERE: Applies to all suppliers engaged by the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the process for managing changes to supplier services, including documentation, risk assessment, and approval procedures. It ensures that all changes are systematically evaluated and communicated.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document and assess all proposed changes to supplier services. WHO: Change management team and relevant stakeholders are responsible for evaluation. WHEN: Changes must be documented within 48 hours of proposal. HOW: Follow the established change management process, including risk assessment and approval workflows. WHERE: Applies to all supplier services across the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for assessing cloud service providers to ensure they meet the organization's security standards. It includes criteria for evaluating security controls, compliance certifications, and incident response capabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a comprehensive assessment of potential cloud service providers before engagement. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for conducting the assessment. WHEN: Assessments must be completed prior to contract signing and reviewed annually. HOW: Use a standardized assessment checklist based on ISO/IEC 27002 criteria. WHERE: Applicable to all cloud service providers considered for organizational use.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the encryption of sensitive data stored and transmitted in cloud services to protect against unauthorized access and data breaches.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement encryption for all sensitive data in transit and at rest within cloud services. WHO: The IT Department is responsible for implementing encryption protocols. WHEN: Encryption must be applied before any sensitive data is uploaded to cloud services. HOW: Utilize industry-standard encryption algorithms and key management practices. WHERE: This applies to all cloud services used by the organization that handle sensitive data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for evaluating customer-initiated issues to determine if they constitute an information security incident. It includes criteria for escalation and communication with customers regarding incident status and resolution.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish criteria for evaluating customer-initiated issues as security incidents and define escalation procedures. WHO: The Incident Response Team is responsible for implementing these procedures. WHEN: Evaluation must occur within 1 hour of issue reporting, with escalation initiated if service levels are not met within 4 hours. HOW: Use a standardized incident evaluation form and escalation matrix to guide the process. WHERE: This control applies to all customer service interactions and incident management systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for classifying security events based on their severity and potential impact on the organization and its customers. It includes criteria for determining whether an event is classified as an incident and the necessary steps for escalation.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Classify security events based on predefined criteria. WHO: The Information Security Officer (ISO) is responsible for classification. WHEN: Classification must occur within 24 hours of event detection. HOW: Utilize the event classification matrix aligned with ISO/IEC 27002 standards. WHERE: Applicable to all information security events reported through the incident management system.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the decision-making process for responding to classified security incidents, including roles, responsibilities, and communication protocols.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a decision-making framework for incident response. WHO: The Incident Response Team (IRT) will make decisions based on event classification. WHEN: Decisions must be made within 48 hours of classification. HOW: Follow the incident response plan and utilize decision trees for response options. WHERE: Decisions are made in the incident response command center or via secure communication channels.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for reporting information security incidents and communicating with affected stakeholders. It specifies the procedures for documenting incidents and the criteria for notifying customers based on the severity and impact of the incident.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Employees must report incidents to the incident response team using the designated reporting tool. WHO: All employees and contractors are responsible for reporting incidents. WHEN: Reports must be made immediately upon discovery of an incident. HOW: Use the incident reporting system to document the incident details, including time, nature, and impact. WHERE: This applies to all organizational units and locations where incidents may occur.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the steps for conducting post-incident reviews, including the collection of incident data and stakeholder engagement. It specifies the roles and responsibilities of involved parties and the timeline for conducting reviews.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a post-incident review meeting with affected stakeholders to discuss the incident and gather insights. Collect incident data, including hours spent and costs incurred. WHO: The incident response team will lead the review, with participation from affected departments and customer representatives. WHEN: The review must be conducted within one week of the incident resolution. HOW: Utilize a structured meeting agenda to guide discussions and document findings. WHERE: The review will take place in a designated meeting room or via a virtual meeting platform, ensuring all stakeholders can participate.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific procedures for handling, documenting, and storing evidence collected during investigations. It includes guidelines for the chain of custody, evidence labeling, and secure storage requirements.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement procedures for evidence handling, including documentation and storage. WHO: All personnel involved in evidence collection and management. WHEN: Procedures must be followed at all times during evidence collection and handling. HOW: Use standardized forms for documentation and secure storage facilities for evidence. WHERE: Applies to all locations where evidence is collected and stored, including on-premises and cloud environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for developing, implementing, and maintaining a disruption response plan that addresses potential threats to information security. It includes guidelines for risk assessment, resource allocation, and communication strategies during disruptions.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and maintain a disruption response plan that includes risk assessment and communication strategies. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for drafting and updating the plan, while department heads must ensure their teams are trained. WHEN: The plan must be reviewed and updated annually or after any significant disruption. HOW: Use a standardized template for the plan and conduct training sessions to ensure understanding.
