WHERE: The plan applies to all organizational units and locations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for developing a disaster recovery plan specifically for telecommunications services. It includes guidelines for assessing risks, identifying critical services, and establishing recovery time objectives (RTOs) and recovery point objectives (RPOs). The plan must be regularly tested and updated to ensure effectiveness.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and implement a disaster recovery plan for telecommunications services. WHO: The IT Disaster Recovery Team is responsible for creating and maintaining the plan. WHEN: The plan must be reviewed and tested annually, with updates made as necessary. HOW: Utilize risk assessment tools and recovery planning frameworks to identify critical services and establish RTOs and RPOs. WHERE: The plan applies to all telecommunications operating areas and systems supporting service delivery.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the protocols for graceful degradation of telecommunications services during emergencies. It specifies the prioritization of services to be maintained and those to be degraded or stopped, ensuring that emergency services remain operational.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish and document service degradation protocols. WHO: The Telecommunications Operations Manager is responsible for implementing these protocols. WHEN: Protocols must be reviewed quarterly and updated based on service performance and incident reviews. HOW: Conduct regular training and simulations to ensure staff are familiar with degradation procedures. WHERE: This applies to all telecommunications service delivery environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for assessing and monitoring compliance with legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements. It includes the identification of applicable laws, regular audits, and reporting mechanisms to ensure ongoing compliance.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct regular compliance assessments and audits to identify applicable legal and regulatory requirements. WHO: The Compliance Officer is responsible for overseeing the assessments, while department heads will assist in identifying relevant regulations. WHEN: Assessments will be conducted annually, with additional reviews triggered by changes in laws or regulations. HOW: Utilize compliance management tools and frameworks to document findings and track compliance status. WHERE: This control applies to all departments and systems within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for identifying and registering intellectual property assets within the organization. It includes guidelines for documenting inventions, trademarks, and copyrights to ensure proper protection.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct regular audits to identify potential intellectual property assets and ensure they are documented and registered. WHO: The Intellectual Property Management Team is responsible for overseeing the identification and registration process. WHEN: Audits will be conducted annually, with immediate registration of new intellectual property as it is created. HOW: Utilize a centralized database to track and manage intellectual property assets. WHERE: This control applies to all departments involved in the creation of intellectual property.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates training programs for employees to enhance their understanding of intellectual property rights and the importance of compliance. It includes the development of training materials and schedules.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and deliver training sessions on intellectual property rights and compliance requirements. WHO: The Human Resources Department, in collaboration with the Legal Department, will be responsible for training program development and delivery. WHEN: Training will be conducted bi-annually, with additional sessions as needed for new hires. HOW: Training effectiveness will be measured through assessments and feedback surveys. WHERE: Training will be conducted in-person and online, accessible to all employees.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the access control measures required to protect organizational records from unauthorized access. It specifies the roles and responsibilities for granting, reviewing, and revoking access to records.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement role-based access controls to restrict access to records based on job responsibilities. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for managing access controls, while department heads are responsible for approving access requests. WHEN: Access reviews will be conducted quarterly, and access will be revoked immediately upon termination of employment. HOW: Access control lists and audit logs will be maintained to track access to records. WHERE: This control applies to all systems and locations where records are stored, including on-premises servers and cloud storage.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the requirements for the retention and disposal of records to ensure compliance with legal and regulatory obligations. It includes guidelines for determining retention periods and secure disposal methods.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish and maintain a record retention schedule that specifies retention periods for different types of records. WHO: The Compliance Officer is responsible for overseeing the retention schedule, while all employees must adhere to it. WHEN: The retention schedule will be reviewed annually, and records will be disposed of securely at the end of their retention period. HOW: Secure disposal methods, such as shredding for paper records and data wiping for electronic records, will be employed. WHERE: This control applies to all physical and digital records across the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the access control measures required to protect PII from unauthorized access. It specifies the roles and responsibilities for granting, reviewing, and revoking access to PII, ensuring that only authorized personnel can access sensitive information.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement role-based access controls to restrict access to PII. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for managing access controls, while department heads must approve access requests. WHEN: Access reviews will be conducted quarterly, and access will be revoked immediately upon employee termination or role change. HOW: Access will be managed through an identity and access management system that logs all access attempts. WHERE: This control applies to all systems and applications that store or process PII.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the encryption standards for protecting PII during storage and transmission. It mandates the use of encryption technologies to safeguard sensitive data against unauthorized access and breaches.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All PII must be encrypted using industry-standard encryption algorithms both at rest and in transit. WHO: The IT Department is responsible for implementing encryption technologies, while all employees must adhere to these standards. WHEN: Encryption must be applied at the time of data collection and maintained throughout the data lifecycle. HOW: Encryption will be enforced through automated tools and regular audits to ensure compliance. WHERE: This applies to all databases, file systems, and communication channels that handle PII.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for conducting independent reviews of information security practices, including the selection of reviewers, review methodologies, and reporting requirements.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct independent reviews of information security practices. WHO: Information Security Officer (ISO) and external auditors. WHEN: Annually, or as required by changes in the security landscape. HOW: Utilize established review methodologies aligned with ISO/IEC 27002 standards. WHERE: Across all organizational units and systems involved in information security.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for training and awareness programs related to information security policies and standards. All employees must participate in training sessions and demonstrate understanding of the policies to ensure compliance.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct annual training sessions on information security policies and standards. WHO: The Information Security Officer is responsible for organizing and delivering the training. WHEN: Training sessions will be held annually, with additional sessions scheduled as needed for new hires. HOW: Training effectiveness will be measured through assessments and feedback surveys. WHERE: Training will be conducted in-person or via an online learning platform accessible to all employees.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the processes for monitoring compliance with information security policies and reporting non-compliance incidents. Regular audits and assessments will be conducted to ensure adherence to standards.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Perform bi-annual audits of compliance with information security policies. WHO: The Compliance Officer will lead the audit process, with support from the IT department. WHEN: Audits will be conducted every six months, with reports generated within two weeks of completion. HOW: Compliance will be assessed against established criteria, and findings will be documented and reviewed. WHERE: Audits will cover all organizational units and systems that handle sensitive information.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific requirements for documenting incident handling procedures, including the criteria for invoking emergency or crisis protocols. It ensures that all personnel are trained and aware of the procedures to follow in the event of a customer-initiated issue.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document and maintain incident handling procedures that specify criteria for invocation. WHO: Operational managers and incident response teams are responsible for documentation and training. WHEN: Procedures must be reviewed and updated quarterly or after any incident. HOW: Use a standardized template for documentation and conduct training sessions for all relevant personnel. WHERE: Applicable to all operational units and systems involved in incident management.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for defining boundaries and managing interfaces between telecommunications organizations. It includes guidelines for partitioning systems and isolating networks to prevent unauthorized access and mitigate risks.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Telecommunications organizations must define and document the boundaries and interfaces with other organizations. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for boundary definition, while Network Operations is responsible for interface management. WHEN: This process must be reviewed and updated annually or whenever significant changes occur. HOW: Organizations will use network diagrams and security assessments to define boundaries and manage interfaces. WHERE: This applies to all interconnected telecommunications systems and networks.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Specification of Security Features and Service Levels
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for telecommunications organizations to specify and communicate the security features and service levels associated with their services. It mandates the creation of clear statements regarding these specifications to ensure customer awareness and understanding.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Telecommunications organizations must create and publish a clear statement of security features and service levels for each service offered. WHO: The responsibility lies with the security management team and service delivery managers. WHEN: This must be completed prior to the launch of any new telecommunications service. HOW: The statement should be developed through collaboration with legal, compliance, and technical teams, ensuring it meets all regulatory requirements. WHERE: This applies to all service delivery platforms and customer communication channels.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the implementation of user awareness programs aimed at educating customers about potential threats such as unsolicited communications and cybercrime. It requires organizations to actively engage users in security practices.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Organizations must conduct regular awareness campaigns and training sessions for users. WHO: The responsibility lies with the marketing and security awareness teams. WHEN: Awareness activities should be conducted quarterly and upon the launch of new services. HOW: Methods include workshops, webinars, and distribution of informational materials. WHERE: These activities should be implemented across all customer interaction points, including online platforms and physical locations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the implementation of specific security controls to protect telecommunications services from various threats, including unsolicited communications and denial-of-service attacks.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Organizations must implement technical controls such as firewalls, intrusion detection systems, and anti-malware solutions. WHO: The IT security team is responsible for the implementation and ongoing management of these controls. WHEN: Controls must be implemented prior to service delivery and reviewed annually. HOW: The effectiveness of controls should be measured through regular security assessments and penetration testing. WHERE: These controls must be applied across all telecommunications infrastructure and service delivery platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific procedures for responding to spam incidents reported by service users. It details the steps to be taken when spam is identified, including communication with the offending customer and coordination with other telecommunications organizations.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Telecommunications organizations must establish a process for identifying and responding to spam incidents. WHO: The Customer Service and Security teams are responsible for managing spam complaints and implementing response actions. WHEN: Responses to spam complaints must be initiated within 24 hours of receipt. HOW: Organizations will utilize a standardized incident response template to document actions taken and outcomes. WHERE: This procedure applies to all messaging systems operated by the organization and involves coordination with external telecommunications partners.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for detecting and reporting DoS/DDoS attacks. It includes the use of monitoring tools and the establishment of communication protocols for reporting incidents to the appropriate teams.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement monitoring tools to detect anomalies in network traffic indicative of DoS/DDoS attacks. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for monitoring and reporting incidents. WHEN: Continuous monitoring with immediate reporting upon detection of an attack. HOW: Utilize automated alerts and dashboards to notify the IT Security Team of potential attacks. WHERE: Applies to all network systems and applications within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the strategies and tools to mitigate the impact of DoS/DDoS attacks once detected. It includes the use of firewalls, rate limiting, and traffic filtering.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Deploy firewalls and traffic filtering mechanisms to mitigate the effects of DoS/DDoS attacks. WHO: Network Administrators are responsible for implementing and managing mitigation strategies. WHEN: Activation of mitigation strategies must occur immediately upon detection of an attack. HOW: Utilize predefined rules and configurations in firewalls to block malicious traffic. WHERE: Applies to all network entry points and critical infrastructure.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the recovery procedures following a DoS/DDoS attack and the process for conducting a post-incident review to improve future responses.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Execute recovery procedures to restore services and conduct a post-incident review. WHO: Incident Response Team is responsible for recovery and review processes. WHEN: Recovery actions must begin immediately after the attack is mitigated, with a review conducted within one week of the incident. HOW: Follow the established recovery plan and document findings during the review. WHERE: Applies to all affected systems and services.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for managing confidentiality agreements within telecommunications organizations. It includes guidelines for drafting, reviewing, and enforcing non-disclosure agreements to ensure compliance with the Non-disclosure of Communications Policy.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish and maintain confidentiality agreements for all communications. WHO: Legal and compliance teams are responsible for drafting and reviewing agreements. WHEN: Agreements must be reviewed annually or upon significant changes in operations. HOW: Utilize a standardized template for agreements and maintain a centralized repository for tracking compliance. WHERE: Applicable to all telecommunications facilities and systems handling sensitive communications.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy provides guidelines for securing telecommunications facilities to prevent unauthorized access and ensure the confidentiality of communications. It includes physical and technical security measures.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement physical security measures such as access controls and surveillance systems. WHO: Security personnel and IT staff are responsible for facility security. WHEN: Security measures must be reviewed and tested quarterly. HOW: Conduct regular security audits and vulnerability assessments. WHERE: Applies to all telecommunications facilities and data centers.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the measures to protect user data stored in telecommunications facilities. It includes guidelines for data encryption, access controls, and data integrity checks.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement encryption for all stored user data and establish access controls. WHO: IT security teams are responsible for data protection measures. WHEN: Data protection measures must be implemented immediately upon data storage and reviewed annually. HOW: Utilize encryption technologies and access management tools. WHERE: Applies to all systems and applications storing user data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific protocols for prioritizing and executing essential communications during emergencies. It details the roles and responsibilities of telecommunications personnel and the procedures to follow in coordination with relevant organizations.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement emergency communication protocols that prioritize essential communications. WHO: Telecommunications management and emergency response teams. WHEN: During any declared emergency or natural disaster. HOW: By following established communication procedures and utilizing designated communication channels. WHERE: Across all operational areas of the telecommunications organization, including on-premises and remote locations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy provides guidance on implementing graceful failure measures to ensure that essential communications can continue even during system disruptions. It specifies the order of priority for communication services and the necessary actions to take.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish graceful failure measures for telecommunications services. WHO: Technical staff and system administrators. WHEN: Prior to any anticipated emergency and reviewed regularly. HOW: By conducting risk assessments and implementing redundancy measures for critical communication systems. WHERE: In all telecommunications infrastructure and systems used for essential communications.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures telecommunications organizations must establish for responding to emergencies. It includes guidelines for assessing the necessity and appropriateness of actions taken during emergencies and ensures that legal counsel is consulted prior to implementing any emergency measures.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish and document emergency action procedures that define necessary measures for self-defense and evacuation. WHO: The Compliance Officer is responsible for developing these procedures, with input from legal counsel and operational managers. WHEN: Procedures must be reviewed and updated annually or after any significant incident. HOW: Procedures will be implemented through training sessions and drills, with effectiveness measured through incident response evaluations. WHERE: Applicable across all operational sites and customer service interactions.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for reporting information security incidents and the communication protocols to be followed during incident management. It specifies the roles of personnel responsible for reporting incidents and the channels through which incidents should be communicated.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish a formal incident reporting process that includes a standardized incident report template. WHO: All employees and contractors are responsible for reporting incidents; the IT Security Team is responsible for managing the reporting process. WHEN: Incidents must be reported immediately upon detection, with a maximum reporting window of 1 hour. HOW: Utilize the organization's incident management system to log and track incidents; conduct regular training sessions to ensure awareness of reporting procedures. WHERE: This process applies to all organizational units and locations where incidents may occur.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for implementing organizational controls as specified in ISO/IEC 27002:2022. It includes the establishment of roles and responsibilities, governance structures, and risk management processes to ensure effective oversight of information security.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement organizational controls as per ISO/IEC 27002:2022. WHO: Information Security Officer and Governance Committee. WHEN: Annually reviewed and updated as necessary. HOW: Utilize risk assessment tools and governance frameworks to monitor compliance. WHERE: Across all organizational units and departments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the implementation of people controls, focusing on training, awareness, and personnel security measures to ensure that all employees understand their roles in maintaining information security.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct training sessions and awareness programs on information security. WHO: HR Department and Information Security Officer. WHEN: Bi-annually for all employees. HOW: Use e-learning platforms and in-person workshops to deliver training. WHERE: All organizational locations and remote work environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the physical controls necessary to protect organizational assets, including access controls, surveillance, and environmental security measures.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish physical security measures such as access controls and surveillance systems. WHO: Facilities Management Team. WHEN: Reviewed quarterly and updated as needed. HOW: Conduct physical security audits and assessments. WHERE: All organizational facilities and data centers.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the technological controls required to safeguard information systems, including network security, data encryption, and incident response protocols.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement technological controls such as firewalls, encryption, and intrusion detection systems. WHO: IT Security Team. WHEN: Continuously monitored with quarterly reviews. HOW: Utilize security information and event management (SIEM) tools for monitoring. WHERE: All IT systems and applications.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific security controls that must be implemented within the ISMS to protect telecommunications information. It includes guidelines for risk assessment, control selection, and monitoring of security measures.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a set of security controls based on risk assessments and best practices. WHO: The Information Security Officer (ISO) is responsible for overseeing the implementation. WHEN: Controls must be implemented within 90 days of policy approval and reviewed annually. HOW: Utilize industry-standard frameworks such as ISO/IEC 27001 for control selection and implementation. WHERE: All telecommunications facilities, including data centers, network operations centers, and remote access points.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the procedures for responding to information security incidents within telecommunications organizations. It includes roles, responsibilities, and communication protocols during an incident.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish an incident response team and develop an incident response plan. WHO: The Incident Response Manager will lead the team, supported by IT security personnel. WHEN: The plan must be tested bi-annually and updated as necessary. HOW: Conduct regular training and simulations to ensure readiness. WHERE: All operational areas of the organization, including remote and cloud environments.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Identification and Assessment of Information Assets
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for identifying and assessing information assets within telecommunications organizations. It mandates the creation of an inventory of all information assets and the evaluation of associated security risks to prioritize the implementation of necessary controls.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a comprehensive inventory of all information assets and assess associated security risks. WHO: The Information Security Officer (ISO) is responsible for overseeing the process, with support from IT and departmental managers. WHEN: This assessment must be conducted annually, with updates as necessary following significant changes in the organization. HOW: Utilize risk assessment methodologies and tools to evaluate the security posture of each asset. WHERE: This process applies to all organizational units and information systems within the telecommunications organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the implementation of security controls to mitigate identified risks to information assets. It includes guidelines for deploying technical, administrative, and physical controls to protect against threats such as malware and DoS/DDoS attacks.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement appropriate security controls based on the risk assessment results. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for the deployment of technical controls, while department heads will enforce administrative controls. WHEN: Controls must be implemented within three months of the risk assessment completion. HOW: Follow established security frameworks and best practices to select and implement controls. WHERE: All organizational units and systems identified in the risk assessment must be covered.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the specific control measures to be implemented for protecting sensitive data within telecommunications organizations. It includes guidelines for data encryption, access control, and incident response.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement encryption for all sensitive data in transit and at rest. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for encryption implementation and monitoring. WHEN: Encryption must be applied before data transmission and reviewed quarterly. HOW: Utilize industry-standard encryption protocols and tools for data protection. WHERE: Applies to all data processed, transmitted, or stored across organizational systems and third-party services.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the procedures for responding to information security incidents, including detection, reporting, and mitigation strategies.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish an incident response team to handle security breaches. WHO: The IT Security Manager will lead the incident response team. WHEN: Incidents must be reported immediately upon detection and reviewed within 24 hours. HOW: Follow the incident response plan, including documentation and communication protocols. WHERE: Applies to all organizational units and systems involved in information processing.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the compliance requirements for telecommunications organizations that provide network facilities. It includes standards for infrastructure maintenance, service availability, and regulatory adherence.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Network facility providers must conduct quarterly infrastructure assessments and maintain service availability above 99.5%. WHO: Compliance Officer and Network Operations Team. WHEN: Assessments to be conducted quarterly, with reports submitted within two weeks of completion. HOW: Utilize automated monitoring tools and manual inspections to evaluate infrastructure. WHERE: All network facilities operated by the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the operational standards for network service providers, focusing on connectivity, bandwidth management, and customer service protocols.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Network service providers must ensure bandwidth availability meets customer demand and implement customer service response times of less than 24 hours. WHO: Network Service Management Team. WHEN: Bandwidth assessments to be conducted monthly; customer service response times to be monitored continuously. HOW: Use network performance analytics and customer service tracking systems. WHERE: All operational areas where network services are provided.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes quality assurance measures for application service providers, ensuring the reliability and performance of applications delivered to end-users.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Application service providers must implement performance testing for all applications and achieve a minimum uptime of 99%. WHO: Application Development and Quality Assurance Teams. WHEN: Performance testing to be conducted prior to application deployment and reviewed bi-annually. HOW: Employ automated testing tools and user feedback mechanisms. WHERE: All environments where applications are hosted.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the licensing compliance requirements for content application service providers, focusing on regulatory adherence and content delivery standards.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Content application providers must ensure all content delivered complies with licensing regulations and industry standards. WHO: Legal Compliance Team and Content Management Team. WHEN: Compliance reviews to be conducted annually, with immediate reporting of any non-compliance. HOW: Regular audits and content reviews. WHERE: All platforms where content is distributed.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy sets innovation standards for telecommunications organizations providing industry-oriented services, focusing on the development and deployment of advanced technologies.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Industry-oriented service providers must develop and implement at least two innovative solutions annually that meet industry needs. WHO: Research and Development Team. WHEN: Innovation proposals to be submitted bi-annually, with implementation timelines established. HOW: Utilize project management methodologies and stakeholder feedback. WHERE: All operational areas focused on industry-oriented services.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for identifying, reporting, and responding to information security incidents within telecommunications networks. It establishes roles and responsibilities for incident management and defines the steps for incident detection, analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a structured incident management process for telecommunications security incidents. WHO: The Incident Response Team (IRT) is responsible for managing incidents. WHEN: Incidents must be reported immediately upon detection, with a review conducted weekly. HOW: Utilize incident management tools to track and analyze incidents, and conduct post-incident reviews to improve response strategies. WHERE: This control applies to all telecommunications facilities and systems, including on-premises and cloud environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the access control measures necessary to protect telecommunications systems from unauthorized access. It specifies user authentication, authorization processes, and the management of user privileges.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish role-based access control (RBAC) for all telecommunications systems. WHO: System administrators are responsible for implementing and managing access controls. WHEN: Access reviews must be conducted quarterly, and access rights must be updated within 24 hours of personnel changes. HOW: Use access control lists (ACLs) and authentication mechanisms to enforce access policies. WHERE: This control applies to all telecommunications systems, including network management systems and customer databases.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for continuous monitoring of telecommunications networks to detect and respond to security threats in real-time. It includes the deployment of security monitoring tools and the establishment of monitoring protocols.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement continuous network monitoring using intrusion detection systems (IDS) and security information and event management (SIEM) tools. WHO: The Security Operations Center (SOC) is responsible for monitoring and responding to alerts. WHEN: Monitoring must be conducted 24/7, with daily reviews of security logs. HOW: Utilize automated tools to analyze network traffic and generate alerts for suspicious activities. WHERE: This control applies to all telecommunications networks and infrastructure.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for identifying and classifying all information assets within the organization. It includes guidelines for documenting asset types, ownership, and sensitivity levels to facilitate effective risk management.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a comprehensive inventory of all information assets and classify them based on sensitivity and criticality. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for conducting the inventory, while department heads will provide necessary asset information. WHEN: The inventory shall be conducted annually, with updates as needed. HOW: Utilize asset management tools and databases to document and classify assets. WHERE: This process applies to all organizational units and systems managing information assets.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the process for assessing information security risks associated with identified information assets. It includes methodologies for evaluating threats, vulnerabilities, and potential impacts to prioritize security controls.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Perform risk assessments on all identified information assets to evaluate potential threats and vulnerabilities. WHO: The Risk Management Team will lead the assessments, with input from asset owners and IT Security. WHEN: Risk assessments shall be conducted bi-annually or upon significant changes to assets. HOW: Use standardized risk assessment frameworks and tools to evaluate risks. WHERE: This applies to all information assets across the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for implementing protective controls based on the results of the risk assessments. It includes guidelines for selecting, deploying, and monitoring security measures to safeguard information assets.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement appropriate security controls based on risk assessment outcomes, including technical, administrative, and physical measures. WHO: The IT Security Team will implement controls, with oversight from the Compliance Officer. WHEN: Controls must be implemented within three months of risk assessment completion. HOW: Follow established security control frameworks and best practices for deployment. WHERE: This applies to all systems and environments where information assets are stored or processed.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for managing access to information systems and data. It specifies the processes for granting, modifying, and revoking access rights to ensure that only authorized personnel can access sensitive information.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) to manage user access rights. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for managing access controls. WHEN: Access rights shall be reviewed quarterly and updated as necessary. HOW: Access requests must be submitted through the organization's access management system and approved by the respective department heads. WHERE: This control applies to all information systems and data repositories within the organization.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the procedures for responding to information security incidents. It includes the identification, reporting, and management of incidents to minimize impact and recover from breaches effectively.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish an incident response team (IRT) to handle security incidents. WHO: The IRT will consist of members from IT, Legal, and Communications departments. WHEN: Incidents must be reported immediately upon detection, and a response plan must be initiated within 24 hours. HOW: Incidents shall be documented in the incident management system, and post-incident reviews will be conducted to improve response strategies. WHERE: This subpolicy applies to all organizational units and information systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the implementation requirements for integrating the defined telecommunications terms into organizational practices. It includes training, documentation updates, and communication strategies to ensure all personnel are familiar with the definitions.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct training sessions and update documentation to include the defined telecommunications terms. WHO: The IT department, in collaboration with the HR training team, is responsible for implementation. WHEN: Training sessions will be conducted quarterly, and documentation updates will be completed within one month of policy approval. HOW: Utilize online training modules and distribute updated documentation via the internal communication platform. WHERE: All organizational units involved in telecommunications operations must participate in training and have access to updated documentation.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the process for submitting new abbreviations for approval and the criteria for their acceptance. It ensures that all abbreviations used within the organization are vetted for clarity and relevance.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Employees must submit new abbreviations to the policy management team for review. WHO: All employees and contractors are responsible for submitting requests. WHEN: Requests must be submitted at least two weeks prior to the intended use. HOW: Submissions should include the abbreviation, its full form, and context of use. WHERE: Submissions are to be made via the internal policy management system.
